test_cases.title: Notification delivered to smartwatch within 5 seconds when connected and integration enabled
test_cases.description: Verify smartwatch receives a notification within 5 seconds when a new message arrives and integration is enabled.
preconditions: Smartwatch is paired with the account
Smartwatch is connected to the phone
Smartwatch integration is enabled in the app

steps.description: 1. Trigger a new message in the system for the user.
steps.expected_result: New message is created and available in the system.

steps.description: 2. Start a timer immediately after the message is created.
steps.expected_result: Timer starts at message creation time.

steps.description: 3. Observe the smartwatch for incoming notification.
steps.expected_result: A notification appears on the smartwatch.

steps.description: 4. Stop the timer when the notification appears.
steps.expected_result: Measured time is recorded.

test_cases.expected_result: Notification is displayed on the smartwatch within 5 seconds of message creation.

test_cases.title: Notification content shows sender, timestamp, and preview when opened with multiple new messages
test_cases.description: Verify correct display of sender, timestamp, and preview when user opens the notification with multiple new messages pending.
preconditions: Multiple new messages exist for the user

steps.description: 1. Trigger two or more new messages for the user with known sender, timestamp, and preview text.
steps.expected_result: Multiple new messages are created in the system.

steps.description: 2. Wait for notification to appear on the smartwatch.
steps.expected_result: Smartwatch displays a notification indicating new messages.

steps.description: 3. Open the notification on the smartwatch.
steps.expected_result: Notification details view opens.

steps.description: 4. Verify sender, timestamp, and preview for each message are displayed correctly.
steps.expected_result: Displayed sender, timestamp, and preview match the message data.

test_cases.expected_result: For all new messages, sender, timestamp, and preview are displayed correctly on the smartwatch.

test_cases.title: No smartwatch notification when device is not connected
test_cases.description: Verify that no smartwatch notification is sent if the smartwatch is not connected and user receives an in-app hint.
preconditions: Smartwatch is paired but not connected

steps.description: 1. Disable Bluetooth or disconnect the smartwatch.
steps.expected_result: Smartwatch status shows disconnected.

steps.description: 2. Trigger a new message for the user.
steps.expected_result: New message is created in the system.

steps.description: 3. Observe the smartwatch for notifications.
steps.expected_result: No notification appears on the smartwatch.

steps.description: 4. Open the mobile app.
steps.expected_result: App is accessible.

steps.description: 5. Check for in-app hint message.
steps.expected_result: User sees a hint indicating smartwatch is not connected and notification was not sent.

test_cases.expected_result: No smartwatch notification is sent and an in-app hint is shown.

test_cases.title: No smartwatch notification when integration is disabled
test_cases.description: Verify that no smartwatch notification is sent and user is informed when integration is disabled.
preconditions: Smartwatch integration is disabled in the app

steps.description: 1. Confirm integration toggle is set to disabled.
steps.expected_result: Integration status shows disabled.

steps.description: 2. Trigger a new message for the user.
steps.expected_result: New message is created in the system.

steps.description: 3. Observe the smartwatch for notifications.
steps.expected_result: No notification appears on the smartwatch.

steps.description: 4. Open the mobile app and check for hint message.
steps.expected_result: User sees a hint indicating integration is disabled.

test_cases.expected_result: No smartwatch notification is sent and an in-app hint is shown.

test_cases.title: Boundary condition: notification appears at exactly 5 seconds
test_cases.description: Verify that notification delivery at exactly 5 seconds meets acceptance criteria.

steps.description: 1. Trigger a new message for the user and start timer.
steps.expected_result: Message is created and timer starts.

steps.description: 2. Monitor smartwatch and capture time of notification arrival.
steps.expected_result: Notification arrival time is captured.

steps.description: 3. Verify measured time equals 5 seconds.
steps.expected_result: Measured delivery time is exactly 5 seconds.

test_cases.title: E2E encryption of text message between two registered users
test_cases.description: Verify that text messages are encrypted end-to-end and only the recipient can decrypt
preconditions: Sender and recipient are registered users
Both users have valid encryption key pairs

steps.description: 1. Sender composes a text message to the recipient and sends it
steps.expected_result: Message is sent and appears in sender's conversation as delivered/queued

steps.description: 2. Inspect message payload in transit or server logs (without decryption keys)
steps.expected_result: Payload is encrypted and not readable as plaintext

steps.description: 3. Recipient opens the conversation and views the message
steps.expected_result: Message is decrypted and displayed correctly to recipient

steps.description: 4. Attempt to decrypt the message using sender device without recipient private key
steps.expected_result: Decryption fails or produces unreadable output

test_cases.expected_result: Text message is encrypted end-to-end and only the intended recipient can decrypt and view it

steps.description: 2. Inspect media payload in transit or storage without keys
steps.expected_result: Media content is encrypted and not viewable

steps.description: 3. Recipient opens the conversation and downloads/views the media
steps.expected_result: Media is decrypted and rendered correctly on recipient device

test_cases.expected_result: Media is encrypted end-to-end and only the recipient can view it

test_cases.title: TC1 - Message delivery success rate meets >=99.5% within 5 minutes after network restoration
test_cases.description: Validate delivery success rate under intermittent connectivity with controlled drops and restorations.
preconditions: Test environment supports network drop/recovery simulation
Message sender and receiver services are running
Delivery tracking and timestamps are enabled

steps.description: 1. Configure network simulator to drop connectivity for 60 seconds every 5 minutes for 1 hour
steps.expected_result: Network drop/recovery schedule is applied and confirmed by simulator logs

steps.description: 2. Send 10,000 messages at a steady rate during the test window
steps.expected_result: All 10,000 messages are accepted by the sender service and logged with timestamps

steps.description: 3. Restore network after each drop and continue message sending
steps.expected_result: Network recovery occurs as scheduled and messages continue to be queued and sent

steps.description: 4. Collect delivery timestamps and compute percent delivered within 5 minutes after each restoration
steps.expected_result: Delivery metrics are calculated for all messages affected by disruptions

test_cases.expected_result: At least 99.5% of messages are delivered within 5 minutes after network restoration

test_cases.title: TC2 - Message loss rate <=0.1% across 10,000 messages with intermittent network
test_cases.description: Verify loss rate by reconciling sender and receiver logs under network instability.
preconditions: Sender and receiver logs include unique message IDs
Network simulator can induce intermittent drops

steps.description: 1. Initiate intermittent network drops at random intervals (10-120 seconds) for 2 hours
steps.expected_result: Intermittent drops are observed in network logs

steps.description: 2. Send exactly 10,000 messages with unique IDs during the test window
steps.expected_result: Sender logs record 10,000 unique message IDs

steps.description: 3. After completion, reconcile sender and receiver logs by message ID
steps.expected_result: A comparison report is generated listing delivered and missing IDs

steps.description: 4. Calculate message loss rate
steps.expected_result: Loss rate is computed as missing IDs / 10,000

test_cases.expected_result: Message loss rate is <= 0.1% (no more than 10 lost messages)

test_cases.title: TC3 - Duplicate message rate <=0.05% after network disruptions
test_cases.description: Ensure deduplication logic prevents duplicates under intermittent connectivity.
preconditions: Receiver logs include message IDs and delivery timestamps

steps.description: 1. Simulate repeated network drops and recoveries over 1 hour
steps.expected_result: Network disruptions are recorded and verified

steps.description: 2. Send 10,000 messages with unique IDs
steps.expected_result: Sender logs show 10,000 unique messages sent

steps.description: 3. Extract receiver logs and identify duplicate message IDs
steps.expected_result: A list of duplicate IDs is generated

steps.description: 4. Calculate duplicate rate
steps.expected_result: Duplicate rate is computed as duplicate deliveries / 10,000

test_cases.expected_result: Duplicate rate is <= 0.05% (no more than 5 duplicates)

test_cases.title: TC4 - Boundary: Delivery success rate just above 99.5%
test_cases.description: Validate acceptance threshold at the boundary with expected success rate = 99.5%.
preconditions: Controlled test harness can shape delivery delays

steps.description: 1. Configure delays to ensure exactly 9,950 of 10,000 messages are delivered within 5 minutes after restoration
steps.expected_result: Delivery timing is constrained to achieve the target count

steps.description: 2. Run intermittent network drops and send 10,000 messages
steps.expected_result: Test completes with all messages delivered eventually

steps.description: 3. Compute delivery success rate within 5 minutes after restoration
steps.expected_result: Calculated rate equals 99.5%

test_cases.expected_result: Test passes when delivery success rate is exactly 99.5%

test_cases.title: TC5 - Negative: Delivery success rate below 99.5% is rejected
test_cases.description: Ensure system fails the acceptance criterion when delivery success rate falls below target.
preconditions: Controlled test harness can reduce delivery success

steps.description: 1. Configure network disruptions to delay deliveries so only 9,930 of 10,000 messages are delivered within 5 minutes
steps.expected_result: Delivery timings reflect reduced success rate

steps.description: 2. Send 10,000 messages during the test window
steps.expected_result: All messages are sent and logged by sender

steps.description: 3. Compute delivery success rate within 5 minutes after restoration
steps.expected_result: Calculated rate equals 99.3%

test_cases.expected_result: Test fails acceptance criterion because delivery success rate is below 99.5%

test_cases.title: TC6 - Boundary: Message loss rate at 0.1%
test_cases.description: Validate loss rate threshold with exactly 10 lost messages out of 10,000.
preconditions: Ability to induce controlled message loss

steps.description: 1. Induce controlled message loss for exactly 10 message IDs
steps.expected_result: Sender logs show 10,000 sent; receiver logs will exclude 10 IDs

steps.description: 2. Reconcile sender and receiver logs
steps.expected_result: Report shows 10 missing IDs

steps.description: 3. Calculate loss rate
steps.expected_result: Loss rate equals 0.1%

test_cases.expected_result: Test passes when loss rate is exactly 0.1%

test_cases.title: TC7 - Negative: Message loss rate above 0.1% is rejected
test_cases.description: Ensure failure when loss exceeds allowed threshold.

steps.description: 1. Induce controlled loss of 15 messages out of 10,000
steps.expected_result: Sender logs show 10,000 sent; receiver logs missing 15 IDs

steps.description: 2. Reconcile logs and compute loss rate
steps.expected_result: Loss rate equals 0.15%

test_cases.expected_result: Test fails acceptance criterion because loss rate exceeds 0.1%

test_cases.title: TC8 - Boundary: Duplicate rate at 0.05%
test_cases.description: Validate duplicate threshold with exactly 5 duplicates out of 10,000.
preconditions: Ability to induce duplicates (e.g., retry storms)

steps.description: 1. Induce retries to create exactly 5 duplicate deliveries among 10,000 messages
steps.expected_result: Receiver logs include 5 duplicate message IDs

steps.description: 2. Calculate duplicate rate
steps.expected_result: Duplicate rate equals 0.05%

test_cases.expected_result: Test passes when duplicate rate is exactly 0.05%

test_cases.title: TC9 - Negative: Duplicate rate above 0.05% is rejected
test_cases.description: Ensure failure when duplicate rate exceeds allowed threshold.

steps.description: 1. Induce retries to create 8 duplicates among 10,000 messages
steps.expected_result: Receiver logs include 8 duplicate message IDs

steps.description: 2. Calculate duplicate rate
steps.expected_result: Duplicate rate equals 0.08%

test_cases.expected_result: Test fails acceptance criterion because duplicate rate exceeds 0.05%

test_cases.title: TC10 - Recovery window boundary: 5-minute delivery cutoff

steps.description: 1. Use Tab/Shift+Tab to move through focusable elements in the flow
steps.expected_result: Focus moves sequentially through all interactive elements

steps.description: 2. Use Enter/Space/Arrow keys to activate controls and navigate the flow
steps.expected_result: All controls are operable and actions are performed successfully

steps.description: 3. Complete the flow using only the keyboard
steps.expected_result: Flow completes without requiring mouse input

test_cases.expected_result: 100% of interactive elements are reachable and operable via keyboard

test_cases.title: Keyboard trap detection (negative)
test_cases.description: Ensure no keyboard focus traps exist on modal dialogs and menus
preconditions: Modal dialog or menu component is available

steps.description: 1. Open the modal dialog or menu using keyboard
steps.expected_result: Focus moves into the opened component

steps.description: 2. Use Tab/Shift+Tab to cycle through focusable elements
steps.expected_result: Focus moves through elements and is not trapped

steps.description: 3. Use Escape to close the modal or menu
steps.expected_result: Component closes and focus returns to the triggering element

test_cases.expected_result: No keyboard traps exist and focus is managed correctly

test_cases.title: Screen reader compatibility for critical user flow
test_cases.description: Verify critical user flows are fully usable with a screen reader (NVDA or VoiceOver)
preconditions: NVDA or VoiceOver is installed and enabled

steps.description: 1. Start the screen reader and navigate to the first step of the flow
steps.expected_result: Screen reader announces page title and landmarks correctly

steps.description: 2. Navigate through form controls and interactive elements
steps.expected_result: Each element is announced with correct role, name, and state

steps.description: 3. Complete the flow using screen reader commands
steps.expected_result: Flow completes without inaccessible steps or missing announcements

test_cases.expected_result: Critical user flows are fully usable with a screen reader

test_cases.title: Screen reader detects missing labels (negative)
test_cases.description: Ensure screen reader announces missing labels for inputs and flags usability issues
preconditions: NVDA or VoiceOver is installed and enabled
A test form with an unlabeled input exists

steps.description: 1. Navigate to the unlabeled input with the screen reader
steps.expected_result: Input is announced without a proper accessible name

steps.description: 2. Attempt to understand the input purpose using screen reader output
steps.expected_result: Input purpose is unclear due to missing label

test_cases.expected_result: Unlabeled input is detected as a screen reader usability issue

test_cases.title: Color contrast compliance - automated check pass
test_cases.description: Validate text and UI components meet minimum contrast ratios via automated checks
preconditions: Automated contrast checking tool is configured
List of tested pages and components is defined

steps.description: 1. Run automated contrast checks on all tested pages
steps.expected_result: Checks complete without errors

steps.description: 2. Review reported contrast ratios for normal and large text
steps.expected_result: Normal text meets 4.5:1 and large text meets 3:1

test_cases.expected_result: All text and UI components meet minimum contrast ratios

test_cases.title: Color contrast boundary condition - minimum acceptable ratios
test_cases.description: Verify boundary values 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text are accepted
preconditions: Test components with contrast ratios exactly at 4.5:1 and 3:1 exist

steps.description: 1. Measure contrast for normal text at 4.5:1
steps.expected_result: Normal text is reported as passing

steps.description: 2. Measure contrast for large text at 3:1
steps.expected_result: Large text is reported as passing

test_cases.expected_result: Boundary contrast ratios are accepted as compliant

test_cases.title: Color contrast compliance - failure detection (negative)
test_cases.description: Ensure contrast checks fail when ratios are below minimum thresholds
preconditions: A test component with contrast ratio below 4.5:1 for normal text exists

steps.description: 1. Run contrast check on the low-contrast component
steps.expected_result: Contrast tool reports ratio below threshold

steps.description: 2. Verify failure is documented with location and ratio
steps.expected_result: Report includes the failing element and measured ratio

test_cases.expected_result: Contrast check correctly identifies non-compliant elements

test_cases.title: Screen reader coverage for core flow screens (NVDA)
test_cases.description: Verify all core flow screens expose meaningful labels, roles, and focus order using NVDA and accessibility tree inspection.
preconditions: Core flows identified (e.g., Login, Browse, Checkout)
Test data exists for all flows

steps.description: 1. Open the application home screen and start NVDA.
steps.expected_result: NVDA announces page title and main landmarks.

steps.description: 2. Navigate through the Login flow using NVDA reading commands and tab navigation.
steps.expected_result: All fields and buttons have meaningful labels and correct roles, and focus order matches the visual layout.

steps.description: 3. Navigate through the Browse/Search flow using NVDA.
steps.expected_result: Search input, filters, results, and action buttons are announced with correct roles and labels.

steps.description: 4. Navigate through the Checkout/Submit flow using NVDA.
steps.expected_result: Form fields, totals, and submit controls are announced with correct labels and roles.

steps.description: 5. Inspect the accessibility tree for each screen in the core flows.
steps.expected_result: All interactive elements have non-empty accessible names and appropriate roles; no focus order gaps are found.

test_cases.expected_result: 100% of core flow screens provide meaningful labels, roles, and logical focus order with NVDA.

test_cases.title: Screen reader coverage for core flow screens (VoiceOver)
test_cases.description: Verify all core flow screens expose meaningful labels, roles, and focus order using VoiceOver and accessibility tree inspection.
preconditions: VoiceOver is enabled on macOS or iOS
Core flows identified (e.g., Login, Browse, Checkout)
Test data exists for all flows

steps.description: 1. Open the application home screen and enable VoiceOver.
steps.expected_result: VoiceOver announces page title and main landmarks.

steps.description: 2. Navigate through the Login flow using VoiceOver rotor and tab navigation.
steps.expected_result: All fields and buttons have meaningful labels and correct roles, and focus order matches the visual layout.

steps.description: 3. Navigate through the Browse/Search flow using VoiceOver.
steps.expected_result: Search input, filters, results, and action buttons are announced with correct roles and labels.

steps.description: 4. Navigate through the Checkout/Submit flow using VoiceOver.
steps.expected_result: Form fields, totals, and submit controls are announced with correct labels and roles.

steps.description: 5. Inspect the accessibility tree for each screen in the core flows.
steps.expected_result: All interactive elements have non-empty accessible names and appropriate roles; no focus order gaps are found.

test_cases.expected_result: 100% of core flow screens provide meaningful labels, roles, and logical focus order with VoiceOver.

test_cases.title: Keyboard-only completion for Login flow
test_cases.description: Verify the Login flow can be completed using only keyboard controls.

steps.description: 1. Use Tab to move focus to the username field.
steps.expected_result: Focus is visible on the username field.

steps.description: 2. Type valid username and press Tab to move to the password field.
steps.expected_result: Username is entered and focus moves to the password field.

steps.description: 3. Type valid password and press Tab to reach the Login button.
steps.expected_result: Password is entered and focus is visible on the Login button.

steps.description: 4. Press Enter to submit.
steps.expected_result: Login completes successfully without mouse interaction.

test_cases.expected_result: Login flow is fully completable using keyboard only.

test_cases.title: Keyboard-only completion for Checkout flow
test_cases.description: Verify the Checkout flow can be completed using only keyboard controls, including forms and submission.

steps.description: 1. Use Tab/Shift+Tab to navigate through checkout form fields.
steps.expected_result: Focus is visible on each field in a logical order.

steps.description: 2. Use Space/Enter to select options (e.g., shipping method).
steps.expected_result: Options are selected via keyboard and announced appropriately.

steps.description: 3. Use Tab to reach the Submit/Place Order button.
steps.expected_result: Focus is visible on the Submit/Place Order button.

steps.description: 4. Press Enter to submit the order.
steps.expected_result: Order is submitted successfully without mouse interaction.

test_cases.expected_result: Checkout flow is fully completable using keyboard only.

test_cases.title: Focus visibility and logical order across core flows
test_cases.description: Verify visible focus indicator and logical focus order on all interactive elements in core flows.
preconditions: Core flows are accessible in the test environment

steps.description: 1. Navigate each core flow screen using Tab and Shift+Tab.
steps.expected_result: A visible focus indicator appears on every interactive element.

steps.description: 2. Compare focus traversal order to the visual layout.
steps.expected_result: Focus moves logically top-to-bottom and left-to-right based on layout.

steps.description: 3. Use Enter/Space to activate focused controls.
steps.expected_result: Focused controls activate as expected without requiring a mouse.

test_cases.expected_result: All interactive elements have visible focus indicators and logical focus order in core flows.

test_cases.title: Negative: Missing accessible label on an interactive element
test_cases.description: Verify a missing accessible label is detected by screen reader inspection.
preconditions: A test page contains a known unlabeled interactive element

steps.description: 1. Open the test page with the unlabeled element using NVDA or VoiceOver.
steps.expected_result: Screen reader announces the element without a meaningful label or as 'button' without name.

steps.description: 2. Inspect the accessibility tree for the unlabeled element.
steps.expected_result: Accessible name is empty or missing for the element.

test_cases.expected_result: The issue is detected and logged as a failure against the 100% label coverage target.

test_cases.title: Negative: Non-logical focus order due to hidden or off-screen elements
test_cases.description: Verify focus order anomalies are detected when hidden/off-screen elements receive focus.
preconditions: A test page contains hidden/off-screen focusable elements

steps.description: 1. Use Tab to navigate through the page.
steps.expected_result: Focus unexpectedly moves to a hidden/off-screen element.

steps.description: 2. Compare the focus order with the visual layout.
steps.expected_result: Focus order deviates from the visual order.

test_cases.expected_result: Non-logical focus order is identified and logged as a failure.

test_cases.title: Boundary: First and last focusable elements in core flows
test_cases.description: Verify boundary focus traversal for first and last interactive elements in each core flow screen.
preconditions: Core flows are accessible in the test environment

steps.description: 1. Use Shift+Tab from the first focusable element on a core flow screen.
steps.expected_result: Focus moves to the browser chrome or remains within the page without skipping to non-interactive elements.

steps.description: 2. Use Tab from the last focusable element on the screen.
steps.expected_result: Focus moves to the next logical screen element or exits the page without looping incorrectly.

test_cases.expected_result: Focus traversal behaves correctly at first and last focusable elements across core flow screens.

test_cases.title: Keyboard-only modal interaction
test_cases.description: Verify modals in core flows are fully operable using keyboard and focus is trapped correctly.
preconditions: A modal appears in a core flow (e.g., confirmation or details modal)

steps.description: 1. Open the modal using keyboard (Enter/Space).
steps.expected_result: Modal opens and focus moves to the first interactive element inside the modal.

steps.description: 2. Use Tab/Shift+Tab within the modal.
steps.expected_result: Focus is trapped within the modal controls.

steps.description: 3. Use Enter/Space to activate the primary action and close the modal.
steps.expected_result: Action executes and focus returns to the triggering element or a logical next element.

test_cases.expected_result: Modal is fully usable via keyboard with correct focus management.

test_cases.title: API Linting & Schema Validation - 100% Compliance (Positive)
test_cases.description: Verify all published APIs conform to approved standard schema and style guide under automated linting and schema validation.
preconditions: API standards checklist and style guide are approved and accessible
Automated linting and schema validation tools are configured in CI
Published API specifications are available in the repository

steps.description: 1. Trigger the automated linting and schema validation pipeline for all published APIs.
steps.expected_result: Pipeline starts and targets all published API specs.

steps.description: 2. Collect linting and schema validation results for each API spec.
steps.expected_result: All APIs report zero linting/schema violations.

steps.description: 3. Calculate API specification compliance rate.
steps.expected_result: Compliance rate equals 100%.

test_cases.expected_result: All published APIs fully conform to the standard schema and style guide with 100% compliance.

test_cases.title: API Linting & Schema Validation - Non-Compliant Spec (Negative)
test_cases.description: Ensure non-compliant API specs are detected and compliance rate is below 100%.
preconditions: API standards checklist and style guide are approved and accessible
Automated linting and schema validation tools are configured in CI
At least one API spec contains a known deviation from the standard

steps.description: 1. Trigger the automated linting and schema validation pipeline.
steps.expected_result: Pipeline starts and includes the non-compliant API spec.

steps.description: 2. Review linting and schema validation output for the non-compliant API.
steps.expected_result: At least one violation is reported for the non-compliant API spec.

steps.description: 3. Calculate API specification compliance rate.
steps.expected_result: Compliance rate is less than 100% and identifies the failing API.

test_cases.expected_result: Non-compliant APIs are detected, and compliance does not meet the 100% target.

test_cases.title: Contract Tests - All API Versions Pass (Positive)
test_cases.description: Verify 100% contract test pass rate for request/response formats and error handling across all API versions.
preconditions: Contract tests exist for each API version
CI is configured to run contract tests

steps.description: 1. Trigger CI pipeline to run contract tests for all API versions.
steps.expected_result: Contract tests start for each API version.

steps.description: 2. Execute contract tests covering request/response formats and error handling.
steps.expected_result: All tests pass for each API version.

steps.description: 3. Calculate contract test pass rate.
steps.expected_result: Pass rate equals 100%.

test_cases.expected_result: All APIs pass contract tests with a 100% pass rate.

test_cases.title: Contract Tests - Error Handling Mismatch (Negative)
test_cases.description: Ensure contract tests fail when an API version returns non-standard error formats.
preconditions: Contract tests exist for each API version
One API version is configured to return a non-standard error format
CI is configured to run contract tests

steps.description: 1. Trigger CI pipeline to run contract tests for all API versions.
steps.expected_result: Contract tests start for each API version.

steps.description: 2. Execute contract tests for error handling.
steps.expected_result: Tests fail for the API version with non-standard error format.

steps.description: 3. Calculate contract test pass rate.
steps.expected_result: Pass rate is less than 100% and identifies the failing API version.

test_cases.expected_result: Contract tests detect non-standard error handling and reduce the pass rate below 100%.

test_cases.title: Documentation Audit - Completeness for All APIs (Positive)
test_cases.description: Verify all APIs include required documentation sections: auth, endpoints, error codes, and versioning.
preconditions: OpenAPI specs are parsable by the audit tool
All published API docs are accessible

steps.description: 1. Run documentation audit using checklist and automated OpenAPI spec parsing.
steps.expected_result: Audit tool processes all APIs successfully.

steps.description: 2. Validate presence of required sections (auth, endpoints, error codes, versioning) for each API.
steps.expected_result: All APIs include all required documentation sections.

steps.description: 3. Calculate standardized documentation completeness.
steps.expected_result: Completeness equals 100%.

test_cases.expected_result: Documentation completeness is 100% across all APIs.

test_cases.title: Documentation Audit - Missing Required Section (Negative)
test_cases.description: Ensure documentation audit detects missing required sections and reduces completeness rate.
preconditions: OpenAPI specs are parsable by the audit tool
At least one API doc is missing an error codes section

steps.description: 1. Run documentation audit using checklist and automated OpenAPI spec parsing.
steps.expected_result: Audit tool processes all APIs successfully.

steps.description: 2. Validate presence of required sections for each API.
steps.expected_result: Audit flags the API missing the error codes section.

steps.description: 3. Calculate standardized documentation completeness.
steps.expected_result: Completeness is less than 100%.

test_cases.expected_result: Documentation audit reports missing sections and fails the 100% completeness target.

test_cases.title: Boundary: No Published APIs
test_cases.description: Verify compliance metrics handling when there are zero published APIs.
preconditions: No APIs are marked as published
Linting, contract tests, and documentation audit tools are available

steps.description: 1. Trigger linting, contract tests, and documentation audit.
steps.expected_result: Tools run and report no applicable APIs.

steps.description: 2. Review compliance metrics output.
steps.expected_result: Metrics handle zero APIs gracefully and do not report 100% by default without evidence.

test_cases.expected_result: System handles the zero-API condition without errors and flags the absence of published APIs.

test_cases.title: Boundary: New API Version Added Requires Standards Conformance
test_cases.description: Verify a newly added API version is included in compliance checks and must meet 100% targets.
preconditions: A new API version is added and marked as published
Linting, contract tests, and documentation audit tools are configured

steps.description: 1. Trigger linting and schema validation for all published APIs including the new version.
steps.expected_result: New API version is included in linting and schema validation.

steps.description: 2. Run contract tests for all API versions including the new version.
steps.expected_result: Contract tests execute for the new version.

steps.description: 3. Run documentation audit for all APIs including the new version.
steps.expected_result: Audit includes the new version and checks required sections.

test_cases.expected_result: New API version is fully validated and must conform to 100% compliance targets.

test_cases.title: Schema Style Guide Enforcement - Naming Convention (Negative)
test_cases.description: Ensure linting flags non-standard naming convention in schema properties.
preconditions: An API spec includes a property name violating the convention

steps.description: 1. Run linting on the API spec with the naming violation.
steps.expected_result: Linting reports a naming convention violation.

steps.description: 2. Review compliance rate calculation.
steps.expected_result: Compliance is less than 100% and identifies the violating API.

test_cases.expected_result: Naming convention violations are detected and compliance does not meet target.

test_cases.title: OpenAPI Spec Parsing Failure Handling (Negative)
test_cases.description: Verify documentation audit fails gracefully when OpenAPI spec is invalid or unparseable.
preconditions: At least one API spec is malformed or unparseable

steps.description: 1. Run documentation audit on all API specs.
steps.expected_result: Audit attempts to parse all specs.

steps.description: 2. Observe parsing results for the malformed spec.
steps.expected_result: Audit reports a parsing error and flags the API.

steps.description: 3. Review documentation completeness result.
steps.expected_result: Completeness is less than 100% and includes the parsing failure.

test_cases.expected_result: Malformed specs are detected, and completeness target is not met.

test_cases.title: Verify functional pass rate >= 95% per major OS version
test_cases.description: Ensure critical and high-priority automated/manual tests meet pass rate threshold on each supported major OS version.
preconditions: Device lab has supported major OS versions for iOS, Android, Windows, macOS
Test suites are tagged by priority and OS

steps.description: 1. Execute critical and high-priority test suites on iOS major versions in the device lab
steps.expected_result: Test execution completes and results are recorded for each iOS version

steps.description: 2. Execute critical and high-priority test suites on Android major versions
steps.expected_result: Test execution completes and results are recorded for each Android version

steps.description: 3. Execute critical and high-priority test suites on Windows major versions
steps.expected_result: Test execution completes and results are recorded for each Windows version

steps.description: 4. Execute critical and high-priority test suites on macOS major versions
steps.expected_result: Test execution completes and results are recorded for each macOS version

steps.description: 5. Calculate pass rate per OS version using (passed / executed) * 100
steps.expected_result: Pass rate is calculated for each OS version

test_cases.expected_result: Each major OS version shows a pass rate of >= 95% for critical and high-priority test cases.

test_cases.title: Negative: Pass rate below 95% is detected and reported
test_cases.description: Validate that the system flags an OS version when pass rate is below threshold.
preconditions: At least one OS version has induced failures in critical/high tests

steps.description: 1. Run critical/high-priority tests on a selected OS version with known failing cases
steps.expected_result: Test execution completes with failures recorded

steps.description: 2. Calculate pass rate for that OS version
steps.expected_result: Pass rate is computed and is below 95%

steps.description: 3. Review test summary dashboard/report
steps.expected_result: OS version is flagged as below threshold

test_cases.expected_result: The system reports pass rate < 95% and indicates non-compliance for that OS version.

test_cases.title: Verify 100% coverage of compatibility matrix across OS versions and top 5 devices
test_cases.description: Ensure executed devices/OS versions match the published compatibility matrix for all major OS versions and top 5 devices by usage.
preconditions: Device usage analytics list top 5 devices per OS

steps.description: 1. Retrieve the compatibility matrix and top 5 devices per OS from usage analytics
steps.expected_result: Matrix and device lists are available for comparison

steps.description: 2. Run the test plan across all devices/OS versions listed in the matrix
steps.expected_result: Execution records include each matrix entry

steps.description: 3. Compare executed device/OS list against matrix requirements
steps.expected_result: Comparison report shows coverage status for each entry

test_cases.expected_result: Coverage equals 100% for all defined major OS versions and top 5 devices per OS.

test_cases.title: Negative: Missing device/OS entry in coverage report
test_cases.description: Validate detection of incomplete device coverage.
preconditions: At least one device/OS combination is not executed

steps.description: 1. Execute test plan excluding one required device/OS combination
steps.expected_result: Execution records are missing the excluded entry

steps.description: 2. Generate device coverage report
steps.expected_result: Report lists executed and missing entries

test_cases.expected_result: Coverage report shows < 100% and flags the missing device/OS combination.

test_cases.title: Verify cross-device critical defect rate <= 2 per release
test_cases.description: Ensure device/OS-specific critical defects are within acceptable limits.
preconditions: Issue tracker is configured with device/OS-specific tags

steps.description: 1. Query issue tracker for device/OS-specific defects tagged as critical in the current release
steps.expected_result: List of critical device-specific defects is returned

steps.description: 2. Count the number of critical device-specific defects
steps.expected_result: Total critical device-specific defect count is computed

test_cases.expected_result: Critical device-specific defect count is <= 2 for the release.

test_cases.title: Negative: Critical device-specific defect rate exceeds threshold
test_cases.description: Validate alerting when critical device-specific defects exceed limit.
preconditions: Issue tracker has more than 2 critical device/OS-specific defects for the release

steps.description: 1. Query issue tracker for critical device/OS-specific defects for the release
steps.expected_result: List includes more than 2 critical defects

steps.description: 2. Generate release quality report
steps.expected_result: Report indicates defect count exceeds threshold

test_cases.expected_result: System flags the release as non-compliant due to > 2 critical device-specific defects.

test_cases.title: Boundary: Pass rate exactly 95% is accepted
test_cases.description: Ensure boundary condition for pass rate acceptance works.
preconditions: Test results for an OS version yield exactly 95% pass rate

steps.description: 1. Calculate pass rate for the OS version with 95% results
steps.expected_result: Pass rate is computed as exactly 95%

steps.description: 2. Review compliance status for the OS version
steps.expected_result: OS version is marked compliant

test_cases.expected_result: Pass rate of exactly 95% is accepted as meeting the target.

test_cases.title: Boundary: Coverage exactly 100% is accepted
test_cases.description: Validate boundary condition for device coverage.
preconditions: All matrix entries are executed with no extras missing

steps.description: 1. Generate device coverage report after full execution
steps.expected_result: Report shows coverage for all matrix entries

steps.description: 2. Verify coverage value
steps.expected_result: Coverage is reported as 100%

test_cases.expected_result: Coverage of exactly 100% is accepted as meeting the target.

test_cases.title: Boundary: Critical device-specific defects exactly 2 are accepted
test_cases.description: Validate acceptance when defect count equals the limit.
preconditions: Issue tracker has exactly 2 critical device/OS-specific defects for the release

steps.description: 1. Query issue tracker for critical device/OS-specific defects
steps.expected_result: List contains exactly 2 critical defects

steps.description: 2. Review release quality status
steps.expected_result: Status indicates compliance

test_cases.expected_result: Defect count of exactly 2 is accepted as meeting the target.

test_cases.title: Negative: Unsupported OS versions excluded from metrics
test_cases.description: Ensure unsupported OS versions do not affect pass rate or coverage calculations.
preconditions: Compatibility matrix excludes certain OS versions
Test executions exist for unsupported OS versions

steps.description: 1. Generate pass rate and coverage metrics for the release
steps.expected_result: Metrics are computed based on supported OS versions

steps.description: 2. Verify inclusion/exclusion list in the report
steps.expected_result: Unsupported OS versions are excluded from calculations

test_cases.expected_result: Metrics are calculated only for supported OS versions and devices.

test_cases.title: Enforce per-user quota at exact limit (single API)
test_cases.description: Verify user cannot exceed configured storage quota when uploading data up to the exact limit.
preconditions: Test user exists with storage quota set to 100 MB

steps.description: 1. Upload files totaling 99 MB via API A.
steps.expected_result: Uploads succeed and total used storage is 99 MB.

steps.description: 2. Upload an additional file of size 1 MB via API A.
steps.expected_result: Upload succeeds and total used storage is exactly 100 MB.

steps.description: 3. Retrieve user storage usage metrics.
steps.expected_result: Reported usage equals 100 MB and does not exceed quota.

test_cases.expected_result: User storage usage meets but does not exceed the configured limit.

test_cases.title: Reject upload exceeding quota by small margin (boundary)
test_cases.description: Verify system rejects an upload that would exceed the quota by 1 byte.
preconditions: Test user exists with storage quota set to 100 MB
User currently has 100 MB of stored data

steps.description: 1. Attempt to upload a file of size 1 byte via API A.
steps.expected_result: Upload is rejected with quota exceeded error code/message.

steps.description: 2. Retrieve user storage usage metrics.
steps.expected_result: Reported usage remains 100 MB.

test_cases.expected_result: System prevents any storage usage beyond the configured limit and returns a clear quota exceeded response.

test_cases.title: Reject upload after limit reached (larger file)
test_cases.description: Verify system rejects uploads when quota already reached regardless of file size.
preconditions: Test user exists with storage quota set to 100 MB
User currently has 100 MB of stored data

steps.description: 1. Attempt to upload a file of size 10 MB via API A.
steps.expected_result: Upload is rejected with quota exceeded error code/message.

steps.description: 2. Verify no new file is created for the user.
steps.expected_result: No new file appears in the user's storage.

test_cases.expected_result: Uploads are blocked when quota is reached and no data is stored.

test_cases.title: Quota enforcement consistency across APIs (REST, SDK, CLI)
test_cases.description: Verify identical quota enforcement across all supported storage APIs/clients.
preconditions: Test user exists with storage quota set to 50 MB
User is authenticated for REST, SDK, and CLI clients

steps.description: 1. Upload 49 MB via REST API.
steps.expected_result: Upload succeeds and usage is 49 MB.

steps.description: 2. Upload 1 MB via SDK client.
steps.expected_result: Upload succeeds and usage is 50 MB.

steps.description: 3. Attempt to upload 1 MB via CLI client.
steps.expected_result: Upload is rejected with quota exceeded error code/message.

steps.description: 4. Attempt to upload 1 MB via REST API.
steps.expected_result: Upload is rejected with quota exceeded error code/message.

test_cases.expected_result: Quota enforcement is consistent and blocks additional storage across all APIs/clients.

test_cases.title: Concurrent uploads hitting quota boundary
test_cases.description: Verify concurrent uploads do not allow total usage to exceed quota.
preconditions: Test user exists with storage quota set to 100 MB

steps.description: 1. Start two concurrent uploads of 60 MB each via API A.
steps.expected_result: At least one upload is rejected or partially fails due to quota limits.

steps.description: 2. Retrieve user storage usage metrics.
steps.expected_result: Reported usage is ≤ 100 MB.

steps.description: 3. Verify error response for rejected upload includes quota exceeded code/message.
steps.expected_result: Rejected upload returns clear quota exceeded response.

test_cases.expected_result: Total usage never exceeds the quota under concurrent operations.

test_cases.title: Quota enforcement with delete and re-upload
test_cases.description: Verify deleting data frees space and allows new uploads within quota.
preconditions: Test user exists with storage quota set to 100 MB
User currently has 100 MB of stored data

steps.description: 1. Delete a file of size 20 MB.
steps.expected_result: File is deleted and usage is reduced to 80 MB.

steps.description: 2. Upload a new file of size 20 MB.
steps.expected_result: Upload succeeds and usage returns to 100 MB.

steps.description: 3. Attempt to upload an additional 1 MB file.
steps.expected_result: Upload is rejected with quota exceeded error code/message.

test_cases.expected_result: Storage usage reflects deletions and quota enforcement remains correct.

test_cases.title: Quota enforcement on object creation without upload (metadata only)
test_cases.description: Verify creating empty objects or metadata-only entries still enforces quota rules if they consume space.
preconditions: Test user exists with storage quota set to 1 MB
User currently has 1 MB of stored data

steps.description: 1. Attempt to create an empty object or metadata-only entry via API A.
steps.expected_result: Operation is rejected with quota exceeded error code/message if it consumes storage.

steps.description: 2. Verify no new object is present in the user's storage.
steps.expected_result: No new object exists.

test_cases.expected_result: Quota enforcement applies to all storage operations that consume space.

test_cases.title: Quota enforcement for large single upload exceeding limit
test_cases.description: Verify system rejects a single upload larger than the user quota.
preconditions: Test user exists with storage quota set to 50 MB
User currently has 0 MB of stored data

steps.description: 1. Attempt to upload a file of size 60 MB via API A.
steps.expected_result: Upload is rejected with quota exceeded error code/message.

steps.description: 2. Retrieve user storage usage metrics.
steps.expected_result: Reported usage remains 0 MB.

test_cases.expected_result: Single uploads larger than the quota are blocked and no data is stored.

test_cases.title: Verify clear error messaging on quota exceedance
test_cases.description: Ensure error response contains expected error code and human-readable message for quota exceedance.
preconditions: Test user exists with storage quota set to 10 MB
User currently has 10 MB of stored data

steps.description: 1. Attempt to upload a 1 MB file via API A.
steps.expected_result: Upload is rejected with HTTP status and error code for quota exceeded.

steps.description: 2. Inspect error payload for message and code.
steps.expected_result: Payload includes clear 'quota exceeded' message and expected code.

test_cases.expected_result: Quota exceedance returns consistent, clear error code and message.

test_cases.title: Quota enforcement across different clients with same user session
test_cases.description: Verify enforcement consistency when the same user uses different clients sequentially.
preconditions: Test user exists with storage quota set to 20 MB
User is authenticated on Web UI and SDK client

steps.description: 1. Upload 15 MB via Web UI.
steps.expected_result: Upload succeeds and usage is 15 MB.

steps.description: 2. Upload 5 MB via SDK client.
steps.expected_result: Upload succeeds and usage is 20 MB.

steps.description: 3. Attempt to upload 1 MB via Web UI.
steps.expected_result: Upload is rejected with quota exceeded error message.

test_cases.expected_result: Quota enforcement is consistent across clients for the same user.

test_cases.title: Accept message payload at exact configured size limit
test_cases.description: Verify that a message with payload size exactly equal to the configured limit is accepted.
preconditions: Message size limit is configured and known

steps.description: 1. Construct a message payload exactly equal to the configured limit.
steps.expected_result: Payload size matches the configured limit.

steps.description: 2. Send the message to the message endpoint.
steps.expected_result: API responds with success status code (e.g., 200/201).

steps.description: 3. Check server logs for processing result.
steps.expected_result: Logs indicate successful acceptance without warnings.

test_cases.expected_result: Message at the exact size limit is accepted and processed successfully.

test_cases.title: Accept message payload just below size limit
test_cases.description: Verify that a message with payload size just below the configured limit is accepted.
preconditions: Message size limit is configured and known

steps.description: 1. Construct a message payload 1 byte less than the configured limit.
steps.expected_result: Payload size is exactly limit minus 1 byte.

steps.description: 2. Send the message to the message endpoint.
steps.expected_result: API responds with success status code (e.g., 200/201).

steps.description: 3. Verify server logs for acceptance.
steps.expected_result: Logs show successful processing without errors.

test_cases.expected_result: Message just below the size limit is accepted.

test_cases.title: Reject message payload just above size limit
test_cases.description: Verify that a message with payload size just above the configured limit is rejected.
preconditions: Message size limit is configured and known

steps.description: 1. Construct a message payload 1 byte greater than the configured limit.
steps.expected_result: Payload size is exactly limit plus 1 byte.

steps.description: 2. Send the message to the message endpoint.
steps.expected_result: API responds with documented error code (e.g., 413 Payload Too Large).

steps.description: 3. Verify server logs for rejection reason.
steps.expected_result: Logs indicate rejection due to size limit.

test_cases.expected_result: Message above the size limit is rejected with correct error code and message.

test_cases.title: Accept media upload at exact configured size limit
test_cases.description: Verify that a media file exactly at the configured size limit uploads successfully.
preconditions: Media upload size limit is configured and known

steps.description: 1. Prepare a media file with size exactly equal to the configured limit.
steps.expected_result: File size matches the configured limit.

steps.description: 2. Upload the media file via the media upload endpoint.
steps.expected_result: API responds with success status code (e.g., 200/201).

steps.description: 3. Verify the file is stored and accessible.
steps.expected_result: File is present in storage and can be retrieved.

test_cases.expected_result: Media file at the size limit uploads and stores successfully.

test_cases.title: Accept media upload just below size limit
test_cases.description: Verify that a media file just below the configured size limit uploads successfully.
preconditions: Media upload size limit is configured and known

steps.description: 1. Prepare a media file 1 byte less than the configured limit.
steps.expected_result: File size is exactly limit minus 1 byte.

steps.description: 2. Upload the media file via the media upload endpoint.
steps.expected_result: API responds with success status code (e.g., 200/201).

steps.description: 3. Verify the file is stored and accessible.
steps.expected_result: File is present in storage and can be retrieved.

test_cases.expected_result: Media file just below the size limit uploads successfully.

test_cases.title: Reject media upload just above size limit
test_cases.description: Verify that a media file just above the configured size limit is rejected gracefully.
preconditions: Media upload size limit is configured and known

steps.description: 1. Prepare a media file 1 byte greater than the configured limit.
steps.expected_result: File size is exactly limit plus 1 byte.

steps.description: 2. Upload the media file via the media upload endpoint.
steps.expected_result: API responds with documented error code (e.g., 413 Payload Too Large).

steps.description: 3. Verify storage behavior.
steps.expected_result: No file is stored and no partial artifacts exist.

test_cases.expected_result: Media file above the size limit is rejected with correct error and no storage side effects.

test_cases.title: Response time SLA at message size limit
test_cases.description: Verify response time does not exceed SLA for message payload at maximum allowed size.
preconditions: Message size limit is configured and known

steps.description: 1. Construct a message payload exactly equal to the configured limit.
steps.expected_result: Payload size matches the configured limit.

steps.description: 2. Send the message and measure response time.
steps.expected_result: Response time is captured for the request.

steps.description: 3. Compare response time with SLA threshold.
steps.expected_result: Measured response time is within SLA.

test_cases.expected_result: System response time stays within SLA at maximum message size.

test_cases.title: Response time SLA at media size limit
test_cases.description: Verify response time does not exceed SLA for media upload at maximum allowed size.
preconditions: Media upload size limit is configured and known

steps.description: 1. Prepare a media file exactly equal to the configured limit.
steps.expected_result: File size matches the configured limit.

steps.description: 2. Upload the media file and measure response time.
steps.expected_result: Response time is captured for the upload.

steps.description: 3. Compare response time with SLA threshold.
steps.expected_result: Measured response time is within SLA.

test_cases.expected_result: System response time stays within SLA at maximum media size.

test_cases.title: Consistent error code/message for oversized message payloads
test_cases.description: Verify that oversized message payloads return consistent documented error codes/messages without service degradation.
preconditions: Message size limit is configured and known

steps.description: 1. Send multiple oversized message payloads (e.g., limit + 1 byte, limit + 1 KB).
steps.expected_result: Each request is sent successfully to the server.

steps.description: 2. Capture API responses for each request.
steps.expected_result: All responses return the documented error code/message.

steps.description: 3. Monitor system health metrics during the test.
steps.expected_result: No degradation or instability is observed.

test_cases.expected_result: Oversized messages are consistently rejected with documented errors and system remains healthy.

test_cases.title: Consistent error code/message for oversized media uploads
test_cases.description: Verify that oversized media uploads return consistent documented error codes/messages without service degradation.
preconditions: Media upload size limit is configured and known

steps.description: 1. Attempt multiple oversized media uploads (e.g., limit + 1 byte, limit + 1 MB).
steps.expected_result: Each upload request is sent successfully to the server.

steps.description: 2. Capture API responses for each upload attempt.
steps.expected_result: All responses return the documented error code/message.

steps.description: 3. Monitor system health metrics during the test.
steps.expected_result: No degradation or instability is observed.

test_cases.expected_result: Oversized media uploads are consistently rejected with documented errors and system remains healthy.

test_cases.title: Verify backup frequency meets 15-minute target for critical data stores (30-day window)
test_cases.description: Validates that backup schedules and logs show backups at least every 15 minutes for critical data stores over the last 30 days.
preconditions: Access to backup schedules and system logs for the last 30 days
Critical data stores are defined and tagged

steps.description: 1. Retrieve backup schedules for all critical data stores for the last 30 days
steps.expected_result: Schedules are retrieved successfully

steps.description: 2. Retrieve backup execution logs for the same period
steps.expected_result: Logs are retrieved successfully

steps.description: 3. Calculate intervals between consecutive backups for each critical data store
steps.expected_result: Intervals are calculated with timestamps

steps.description: 4. Identify any intervals greater than 15 minutes
steps.expected_result: No intervals exceed 15 minutes

test_cases.expected_result: All critical data stores have backup intervals of 15 minutes or less over the last 30 days.

test_cases.title: Negative: Detect backup frequency violation (>15 minutes)
test_cases.description: Ensures the system flags any critical data store with backup intervals exceeding 15 minutes.
preconditions: Access to backup schedules and system logs for the last 30 days
At least one critical data store has a known gap > 15 minutes in logs

steps.description: 1. Retrieve backup execution logs for the critical data store with a known gap
steps.expected_result: Logs are retrieved successfully

steps.description: 2. Calculate time intervals between backups
steps.expected_result: Intervals are calculated correctly

steps.description: 3. Check for any interval greater than 15 minutes
steps.expected_result: An interval greater than 15 minutes is detected and flagged

test_cases.expected_result: Backup frequency violation is identified for the critical data store.

test_cases.title: Verify cross-region replication success rate meets 99.9% monthly target
test_cases.description: Validates that replication job reports and failure logs show >=99.9% successful replications across regions per month.
preconditions: Access to replication job reports and failure logs for the last 30 days
Replication jobs are configured across regions

steps.description: 1. Retrieve all replication job reports for the last 30 days
steps.expected_result: Reports are retrieved successfully

steps.description: 2. Retrieve failure logs for replication jobs
steps.expected_result: Failure logs are retrieved successfully

steps.description: 3. Calculate total replication attempts and successful replications
steps.expected_result: Totals are calculated accurately

steps.description: 4. Compute success rate as (successful / total) * 100
steps.expected_result: Success rate is computed

steps.description: 5. Compare computed success rate to 99.9% threshold
steps.expected_result: Success rate meets or exceeds 99.9%

test_cases.expected_result: Cross-region replication success rate is >= 99.9% for the month.

test_cases.title: Negative: Cross-region replication success rate below 99.9%
test_cases.description: Ensures that success rates below the target are detected and reported.
preconditions: Access to replication job reports and failure logs for the last 30 days
Failure logs indicate enough failures to drop success rate below 99.9%

steps.description: 1. Retrieve replication job reports and failure logs
steps.expected_result: Reports and logs are retrieved

steps.description: 2. Calculate the success rate
steps.expected_result: Success rate is computed accurately

steps.description: 3. Compare the result to the 99.9% threshold
steps.expected_result: Success rate is below 99.9% and flagged

test_cases.expected_result: Replication success rate below 99.9% is identified and reported.

test_cases.title: Verify monthly scheduled restore tests succeed 100% across regions
test_cases.description: Validates that scheduled restore tests complete successfully across regions for the month.
preconditions: Scheduled restore tests are configured across regions
Access to restore test execution results for the last month

steps.description: 1. Retrieve restore test execution results for the last month
steps.expected_result: Results are retrieved successfully

steps.description: 2. Count total restore tests executed
steps.expected_result: Total count is calculated

steps.description: 3. Count successful restore tests
steps.expected_result: Success count is calculated

steps.description: 4. Verify success rate equals 100%
steps.expected_result: Success rate equals 100%

test_cases.expected_result: All scheduled restore tests across regions succeeded for the month.

test_cases.title: Negative: Restore test failure detected in monthly cross-region recovery tests
test_cases.description: Ensures any failed restore test causes the success rate to drop below 100% and is flagged.
preconditions: At least one restore test failure exists in the last month results

steps.description: 1. Retrieve restore test execution results
steps.expected_result: Results are retrieved successfully

steps.description: 2. Identify any failed restore tests
steps.expected_result: At least one failed test is detected

steps.description: 3. Calculate restore test success rate
steps.expected_result: Success rate is below 100%

test_cases.expected_result: Failed restore tests are detected and the overall success rate is not 100%.

test_cases.title: Verify Recovery Point Objective (RPO) is within 15 minutes during restore tests
test_cases.description: Validates that data loss window during restore tests is <= 15 minutes.
preconditions: Restore tests are executed with recorded timestamps
Access to backup timestamps and restore timestamps

steps.description: 1. Retrieve restore test records with last backup timestamp and restore point timestamp
steps.expected_result: Records are retrieved successfully

steps.description: 2. Calculate RPO as difference between restore point and last backup timestamp
steps.expected_result: RPO values are calculated

steps.description: 3. Verify each RPO value is <= 15 minutes
steps.expected_result: All RPO values are within 15 minutes

test_cases.expected_result: RPO is <= 15 minutes for all restore tests.

test_cases.title: Boundary: RPO equals exactly 15 minutes
test_cases.description: Checks the boundary condition where RPO is exactly 15 minutes to ensure it is accepted.
preconditions: At least one restore test record has RPO exactly 15 minutes

steps.description: 1. Retrieve restore test record with RPO exactly 15 minutes
steps.expected_result: Record is retrieved successfully

steps.description: 2. Validate RPO value against threshold
steps.expected_result: RPO equals 15 minutes and is accepted

test_cases.expected_result: RPO of exactly 15 minutes is considered compliant.

test_cases.title: Negative: RPO exceeds 15 minutes during restore test
test_cases.description: Ensures any RPO greater than 15 minutes is flagged as non-compliant.
preconditions: At least one restore test record has RPO greater than 15 minutes

steps.description: 1. Retrieve restore test record with RPO greater than 15 minutes
steps.expected_result: Record is retrieved successfully

steps.description: 2. Compare RPO value to 15-minute threshold
steps.expected_result: RPO exceeds threshold and is flagged

test_cases.expected_result: RPO violation is detected and reported.

test_cases.title: Validate RPO target is documented and approved by primary and secondary stakeholders
test_cases.description: Verify RPO target definition includes documentation and approvals from both primary and secondary stakeholders

steps.expected_result: DR policy document is displayed

steps.description: 2. Locate the section defining RPO target
steps.expected_result: RPO target value and definition are visible

steps.description: 3. Review approval records linked to the RPO target
steps.expected_result: Approval records show primary stakeholder approval

steps.description: 4. Verify secondary stakeholder approval is present for the RPO target
steps.expected_result: Approval records show secondary stakeholder approval

test_cases.expected_result: RPO target is documented and approved by both primary and secondary stakeholders

test_cases.title: Validate RTO target is documented and approved by primary and secondary stakeholders
test_cases.description: Verify RTO target definition includes documentation and approvals from both primary and secondary stakeholders
preconditions: Access to disaster recovery policy repository
User has permission to view policy and approvals

steps.description: 1. Open the disaster recovery policy document
steps.expected_result: DR policy document is displayed

steps.description: 2. Locate the section defining RTO target
steps.expected_result: RTO target value and definition are visible

steps.description: 3. Review approval records linked to the RTO target
steps.expected_result: Approval records show primary stakeholder approval

steps.description: 4. Verify secondary stakeholder approval is present for the RTO target
steps.expected_result: Approval records show secondary stakeholder approval

test_cases.expected_result: RTO target is documented and approved by both primary and secondary stakeholders

test_cases.description: Ensure the system identifies missing secondary approval for RPO target

steps.description: 3. Review approval records for the RPO target

test_cases.expected_result: RPO target does not meet approval requirements and is flagged as non-compliant

test_cases.title: Negative: RTO target not documented
test_cases.description: Ensure missing RTO target definition is detected

RTO target section is absent or empty

steps.expected_result: DR policy document is displayed

steps.description: 2. Search for RTO target definition section
steps.expected_result: RTO target definition section is missing or empty

test_cases.expected_result: RTO target documentation is missing and is flagged as non-compliant

test_cases.title: Verify achieved RPO meets documented RPO during full DR simulation
test_cases.description: Validate that data loss during DR simulation is within the documented RPO target
preconditions: Full DR simulation plan is approved
Baseline data timestamp is recorded before simulation

steps.description: 1. Initiate a full disaster recovery simulation
steps.expected_result: DR simulation begins and system records start time

steps.description: 2. Restore data and services from backups
steps.expected_result: Data restoration process completes successfully

steps.description: 3. Measure data loss by comparing latest restored data timestamp to failure timestamp
steps.expected_result: Achieved RPO is calculated

steps.description: 4. Compare achieved RPO against documented RPO target
steps.expected_result: Achieved RPO is less than or equal to documented RPO

test_cases.expected_result: Achieved RPO meets or is better than the documented RPO target

test_cases.title: Negative: Achieved RPO exceeds documented RPO during DR simulation
test_cases.description: Validate system flags when data loss exceeds RPO target
preconditions: Full DR simulation plan is approved
Simulation configured to cause data loss beyond target

steps.description: 1. Initiate a full disaster recovery simulation
steps.expected_result: DR simulation begins and system records start time

steps.description: 2. Restore data and services from backups
steps.expected_result: Data restoration process completes successfully

steps.description: 3. Measure data loss and compute achieved RPO
steps.expected_result: Achieved RPO is calculated

steps.description: 4. Compare achieved RPO against documented RPO target
steps.expected_result: Achieved RPO is greater than documented RPO

test_cases.expected_result: RPO achievement fails and is flagged as non-compliant

test_cases.title: Verify achieved RTO meets documented RTO during full DR simulation

Monitoring for service availability is enabled

steps.expected_result: DR simulation begins and system records outage start time

steps.description: 2. Restore services and verify availability
steps.expected_result: Services are restored and availability is confirmed

steps.description: 3. Measure time to restore service
steps.expected_result: Achieved RTO is calculated

steps.description: 4. Compare achieved RTO against documented RTO target
steps.expected_result: Achieved RTO is less than or equal to documented RTO

test_cases.expected_result: Achieved RTO meets or is better than the documented RTO target

test_cases.title: Negative: Achieved RTO exceeds documented RTO during DR simulation
test_cases.description: Validate system flags when service restoration exceeds RTO target
preconditions: Full DR simulation plan is approved

steps.description: 1. Initiate a full disaster recovery simulation
steps.expected_result: DR simulation begins and system records outage start time

steps.description: 2. Restore services and verify availability
steps.expected_result: Services are restored and availability is confirmed

steps.description: 3. Measure time to restore service
steps.expected_result: Achieved RTO is calculated

steps.description: 4. Compare achieved RTO against documented RTO target
steps.expected_result: Achieved RTO is greater than documented RTO

test_cases.expected_result: RTO achievement fails and is flagged as non-compliant

test_cases.title: Boundary: Achieved RPO equals documented RPO
test_cases.description: Ensure equality condition passes for RPO target
preconditions: DR simulation configured to achieve RPO exactly equal to target

steps.description: 1. Run full DR simulation
steps.expected_result: DR simulation completes successfully

steps.description: 2. Compute achieved RPO
steps.expected_result: Achieved RPO equals documented RPO

test_cases.expected_result: RPO achievement is marked as compliant when achieved RPO equals documented RPO

test_cases.title: Boundary: Achieved RTO equals documented RTO
test_cases.description: Ensure equality condition passes for RTO target
preconditions: DR simulation configured to achieve RTO exactly equal to target

steps.description: 1. Run full DR simulation
steps.expected_result: DR simulation completes successfully

steps.description: 2. Compute achieved RTO
steps.expected_result: Achieved RTO equals documented RTO

test_cases.expected_result: RTO achievement is marked as compliant when achieved RTO equals documented RTO

preconditions: Access to disaster recovery policy repository
