US-134.name: User Blocking, Reporting, and Moderation Tools
US-134.description:
As a support moderator
I want to review reported users and apply moderation actions such as blocking or dismissing the report
So that to prevent abuse and maintain a safe, compliant communication platform

background.description: the moderator is authenticated and on the moderation queue page

scenarios.name: Block reported user with sufficient evidence
scenarios.description: Valid report is blocked and resolved

steps.description: a report exists in the moderation queue with required evidence for user "u123"
the moderator selects "Block user" and confirms the action
the reported user "u123" is blocked and cannot send messages
the report status is updated to "Resolved - Blocked"

scenarios.name: Dismiss report with insufficient evidence
scenarios.description: Report is dismissed and reporter is notified with reason

steps.description: a report exists in the moderation queue with insufficient evidence for user "u456"
the moderator selects "Dismiss report" and provides reason "Insufficient evidence"
the report status is updated to "Resolved - Dismissed"
the reporter is notified with reason "Insufficient evidence"

scenarios.name: Attempt to block a non-existent user
scenarios.description: System shows error and report remains pending

steps.description: a report exists in the moderation queue for user "u999" whose account no longer exists
the moderator selects "Block user" and submits the action
an error message is shown indicating the user is unavailable
the report status remains "Pending"

scenarios.name: Scenario Outline: Dismiss report requires a non-empty reason
scenarios.description: Validate boundary conditions for dismissal reason

steps.description: a report exists in the moderation queue with insufficient evidence for user "u777"
the moderator selects "Dismiss report" and provides reason "<reason>"
the system response is "<response>"
the report status is "<status>"

examples.response: validation error shown for missing reason
validation error shown for missing reason

scenarios.name: Scenario Outline: Blocking a user updates messaging permission
scenarios.description: Ensure blocked user cannot send messages after action

steps.description: a report exists in the moderation queue with required evidence for user "<user_id>"
the moderator selects "Block user" and confirms the action
the user "<user_id>" is blocked
message send attempt result is "<message_result>"

US-135.name: Typing Indicators and Presence
US-135.description:
As a registered customer
I want to view online status and typing indicators for chat participants and control my visibility settings
So that communicate more efficiently while protecting my privacy

background.description: a one-to-one chat exists between User A and User B
both users are authenticated and have chat open

scenarios.name: Display typing indicator in real time when both users enable indicators
scenarios.description: Validates real-time typing indicator appears and clears on send or stop

steps.description: User A and User B have presence and typing indicators enabled
User B starts typing a message
User A sees a typing indicator for User B in real time
the typing indicator disappears when User B sends the message or stops typing

scenarios.name: Privacy settings hide presence and typing indicators
scenarios.description: Validates invisible status or typing disabled hides presence and typing indicators

steps.description: User B sets their visibility to <visibility_setting>
User B sets typing indicators to <typing_setting>
User A views the chat with User B
User B appears offline to User A
no typing indicator is shown for User B

scenarios.name: Chat remains usable when presence updates fail
scenarios.description: Validates resilience when presence or typing updates cannot be retrieved

steps.description: presence or typing indicator updates cannot be retrieved due to a <error_type>
the system attempts to fetch presence and typing updates
the chat remains usable for sending and receiving messages
no stale or incorrect indicators are displayed

scenarios.name: Typing indicator clears when typing stops within boundary timeout
scenarios.description: Validates boundary condition for typing stop timeout

steps.description: User A and User B have presence and typing indicators enabled
User B stops typing for <idle_seconds> seconds without sending a message
User A no longer sees the typing indicator

US-136.name: Payments and P2P Transfers
US-136.description:
As a registered user
I want to send and receive secure in-app P2P payments and view transaction history
So that to transfer money quickly with confidence and keep an auditable record of all transactions

background.description: the user is registered, verified, and logged into the app
the user has a valid payment method on file

scenarios.name: Successful P2P payment completes and appears in history
scenarios.description: Valid payment between two registered users completes successfully

steps.description: the sender has an available balance of 100.00
the recipient is a registered user
the sender sends 25.00 to the recipient
the payment is completed
both users receive a confirmation message
the transaction appears in both users' history with status Completed

scenarios.name: Compliance verification required blocks payment
scenarios.description: Payment is blocked when compliance flow requires extra verification

steps.description: the payment triggers compliance checks
the compliance flow requires additional verification
the app prompts for the required information
the payment remains blocked until verification is completed
no transaction is recorded in history

scenarios.name: Payment rejected when amount exceeds available balance
scenarios.description: Insufficient balance causes rejection with no history record

steps.description: the sender has an available balance of 20.00
the sender attempts to send 25.00
the payment is rejected with a clear error message
no transaction is recorded

scenarios.name: Transaction history displays all required fields
scenarios.description: History screen lists transactions with status, amount, counterparty, and timestamp

steps.description: the user has completed and failed transactions in history
the user opens the transaction history screen
each transaction shows status, amount, counterparty, and timestamp
failed transactions are clearly marked

scenarios.name: Boundary: Send amount equal to available balance
scenarios.description: Exact balance transfer should succeed

steps.description: the sender has an available balance of <balance>
the recipient is a registered user
the sender sends <amount>
the payment is completed
the transaction appears in both users' history with status Completed

scenarios.name: Edge: Minimum transfer amount enforced
scenarios.description: System validates the minimum allowed transfer amount

steps.description: the sender has an available balance of 100.00
the minimum transfer amount is <min_amount>
the sender attempts to send <amount>
the payment is rejected with a clear error message
no transaction is recorded

US-137.name: Voice and Video Calling
US-137.description:
As a primary user
I want to start a one-to-one or group voice/video call and control mute, camera, and speaker during the call
So that to communicate reliably and intuitively across platforms with good call quality and privacy

background.description: the user is authenticated and on the call screen

scenarios.name: Start a call and control mute, camera, and speaker
scenarios.description: Validates successful call connection and availability of controls for one-to-one and group calls

steps.description: the user has granted microphone and camera permissions
the user is connected to the internet
the user initiates a <call_type> call with <participant_count> available participants
the call connects successfully
the user can see and use mute, camera on/off, and speaker controls

scenarios.name: Adapt quality under degraded network conditions
scenarios.description: Ensures quality adaptation and indicator display when bandwidth drops during an active call

steps.description: an active <call_type> call is in progress
the user is connected to the internet with <initial_bandwidth> bandwidth
network bandwidth degrades to <degraded_bandwidth>
the system adapts audio or video quality to maintain continuity
a call quality indicator is shown to the user

scenarios.name: Prevent call start when participant is unavailable
scenarios.description: Validates clear error handling when participants are unavailable

steps.description: the user has granted microphone and camera permissions
the user is connected to the internet
the user initiates a <call_type> call with <participant_count> participants and at least one is unavailable
the system displays a clear error message
the call does not start

scenarios.name: Block call when permissions are denied
scenarios.description: Ensures call cannot start without required permissions and shows error

steps.description: microphone permission is <mic_permission> and camera permission is <cam_permission>
the user is connected to the internet
the user initiates a <call_type> call with available participants
the system displays a clear error message about permissions
the call does not start

US-138.name: Enterprise/Admin Analytics and Audit Logs
US-138.description:
As a system administrator
I want to view system-level analytics and audit logs for message throughput and compliance events
So that ensure reliable performance, detect issues early, and meet compliance requirements

background.description: the analytics and audit logging services are available

scenarios.name: View analytics dashboard for a valid time range
scenarios.description: Valid admin views throughput, delivery rates, and system health for a selected period

steps.description: the administrator has valid admin permissions
they open the analytics dashboard and select a time range
the system displays throughput metrics, delivery rates, and system health indicators for that period
no stale data is displayed from outside the selected time range

scenarios.name: Filter audit logs by user, event type, and date range
scenarios.description: Valid admin filters audit logs and receives matching entries with required fields

steps.description: audit logging is enabled and events exist
the administrator filters audit logs by user, event type, and date range
the system returns matching log entries with timestamps, actor, action, and outcome
the returned entries match the selected filters

scenarios.name: Analytics empty state for no data in selected range
scenarios.description: Empty state is shown when no analytics data exists for the range

steps.description: the administrator has valid admin permissions
no analytics data exists for the selected time range
they request analytics for that time range
the system shows an empty state message
the system does not display incorrect or stale data

scenarios.name: Audit logs empty state for no matching events
scenarios.description: Empty state is shown when no logs match the selected filters

steps.description: audit logging is enabled
no audit events match the selected filters
the administrator requests audit logs with those filters
the system shows an empty state message
the system does not display incorrect or stale data

scenarios.name: Access denied for insufficient permissions
scenarios.description: User without admin permissions is blocked from analytics and audit logs

steps.description: the user does not have sufficient permissions
they request the analytics dashboard or audit logs
the system denies access
an authorization error is displayed

scenarios.name: Analytics time range boundary conditions
scenarios.description: Validate analytics behavior at boundary time ranges

steps.description: the administrator has valid admin permissions
they select a time range from <start> to <end>
the system displays metrics for that exact period only

scenarios.name: Audit log filter boundary conditions
scenarios.description: Validate audit log filtering at boundary timestamps and multiple filter values

steps.description: audit logging is enabled and events exist at boundary timestamps
the administrator filters logs for user <user>, event type <event_type>, from <start> to <end>
the system returns entries within the inclusive boundary range
each entry includes timestamp, actor, action, and outcome

US-139.name: Third-Party Integrations and Bots - Webhook Registration
US-139.description:
As a technical integration engineer
I want to register and configure webhook endpoints for third-party bot integrations via the API
So that enable automated and reliable message processing while maintaining secure external connectivity

background.description: the API endpoint for webhook registration is available
the integration engineer has a valid API key with integration permissions

scenarios.name: Register webhook successfully and receive test delivery
scenarios.description: Valid registration creates a webhook and triggers a test delivery

steps.description: a valid webhook URL and supported event types are prepared
the engineer submits a webhook registration request
the API creates the webhook
a test event delivery is sent to the webhook endpoint successfully

scenarios.name: Register webhook with multiple event types using scenario outline
scenarios.description: Data-driven validation for supported event type combinations

steps.description: a valid webhook URL "<webhook_url>"
event types "<event_types>" are supported by the API
the engineer registers the webhook
the webhook is created with event types "<event_types>"
the test event delivery succeeds

scenarios.name: Reject invalid webhook URL format
scenarios.description: Invalid URL formats are rejected with validation errors

steps.description: an invalid webhook URL "<webhook_url>"
supported event types "<event_types>"
the engineer attempts to register the webhook
the API responds with a validation error
no webhook is created

scenarios.name: Rate limit exceeded on webhook registration
scenarios.description: Excess requests return rate limit error with retry-after

steps.description: the integration has reached the allowed rate limit
the engineer submits another webhook registration request
the API responds with a rate limit error
the response includes retry-after information

scenarios.name: Boundary condition for maximum allowed event types
scenarios.description: Registration with the maximum number of event types is accepted

steps.description: a valid webhook URL "<webhook_url>"
the request includes the maximum allowed number of event types "<event_types>"
the engineer registers the webhook
the API creates the webhook
a test event delivery is sent successfully

US-140.name: Verify Message Send Latency
US-140.description:
As a QA Performance Engineer
I want to validate message send and delivery acknowledgement latency under normal load
So that ensures user messaging experience meets strict latency targets and SLA commitments

background.description: the performance test environment is configured with normal load profile and synchronized clocks

scenarios.name: P95 latency meets targets under normal load (happy path)
scenarios.description: Validates P95 send and delivery acknowledgement latency within SLA at normal load

steps.description: the system is running a normal load test with steady state traffic
P95 send latency and P95 delivery acknowledgement latency are computed from end-to-end traces
P95 send latency is less than or equal to 200 ms
P95 delivery acknowledgement latency is less than or equal to 500 ms

scenarios.name: P99 latency meets targets under normal load (boundary conditions)
scenarios.description: Validates P99 send and delivery acknowledgement latency at boundary thresholds

steps.description: the system is collecting latency distribution during a normal load test
P99 send latency and P99 delivery acknowledgement latency are computed
P99 send latency is less than or equal to 400 ms
P99 delivery acknowledgement latency is less than or equal to 800 ms

scenarios.name: Latency measurements at threshold values (edge case outline)
scenarios.description: Ensures pass/fail behavior exactly at SLA thresholds

steps.description: the system is running a normal load test with controlled latency injection
the computed percentile latency for <metric> equals <value_ms> ms
the <metric> result is accepted as within SLA

scenarios.name: Latency targets violated under normal load (error scenario outline)
scenarios.description: Detects SLA breach when percentiles exceed targets

steps.description: the system is running a normal load test with performance degradation
the computed percentile latency for <metric> is <value_ms> ms
the <metric> result is flagged as SLA breach

US-141.name: US-141 Verify Media Upload Throughput
US-141.description:
As a Performance QA Engineer
I want to Validate media upload throughput and completion times under expected and peak load
So that Ensures users experience reliable and predictable upload performance

background.description: a controlled performance test environment with network instrumentation enabled

scenarios.name: Meet throughput targets under expected and peak load
scenarios.description: Validates average per-client upload throughput for voice and media files meets targets

steps.description: representative voice and media files are prepared for upload
run load tests at <load_level> and measure average throughput per client
the average upload throughput is at least <min_mbps> Mbps
network instrumentation confirms stable client-side transfer rates

scenarios.name: 95th percentile completion time meets expected load target
scenarios.description: Validates 95th percentile completion time for 10 MB file under expected load

steps.description: timed uploads are executed at expected load
measure the 95th percentile completion time for a 10 MB file
the 95th percentile completion time is at most 20 seconds
all timing data is captured consistently across clients

scenarios.name: 99th percentile completion time boundary at peak load
scenarios.description: Validates 99th percentile completion time boundary for 10 MB file under peak load

steps.description: stress tests run with concurrent uploads at peak load
measure the 99th percentile completion time for a 10 MB file
the 99th percentile completion time is at most <max_seconds> seconds
completion time distribution shows no outliers above the boundary

scenarios.name: Upload success rate failure under sustained load
scenarios.description: Negative scenario validating failure when success rate drops below target

steps.description: a 1-hour sustained concurrency test is executed
calculate the upload success rate from total attempts and failures
the success rate is below 99.5%
the test is marked as failed due to reliability threshold breach

US-142.name: US-142 Verify Concurrent User Scaling
US-142.description:
As a QA Engineer
I want to verify horizontal scaling maintains low latency under high concurrent user loads
So that ensures the system can handle peak demand without degrading user experience or violating performance expectations

background.description: the load test environment is provisioned with monitoring for latency, throughput, and error rates

scenarios.name: Sustained load at 10,000 users meets latency, throughput, and error targets
scenarios.description: Happy path validating all acceptance criteria under peak concurrency

steps.description: the system is running with N instances and a ramp-up to 10,000 concurrent users
the test harness measures p95 latency, throughput, and error rate at steady state
p95 response time is less than or equal to 500 ms
throughput is greater than or equal to 5,000 requests per second
error rate is less than or equal to 0.5 percent

scenarios.name: Boundary conditions at exact threshold values
scenarios.description: Boundary validation when metrics are exactly at target thresholds

steps.description: the system is running at 10,000 concurrent users with stable load
the test harness records the boundary values for p95 latency, throughput, and error rate
p95 response time is less than or equal to <p95_ms> ms
throughput is greater than or equal to <rps> requests per second
error rate is less than or equal to <error_rate_percent> percent

scenarios.name: Scaling from N to 2N instances keeps latency increase within 10 percent
scenarios.description: Edge case verifying latency impact when doubling instances under identical load

steps.description: the system is running with N instances at 10,000 concurrent users
the system is scaled to 2N instances under the same load and p95 latency is re-measured
the p95 latency increase is less than or equal to <max_increase_percent> percent

scenarios.name: Failure when performance targets are exceeded
scenarios.description: Error scenario where latency, throughput, or error rate violates targets

steps.description: the system is under peak load at 10,000 concurrent users
the test harness records metrics that exceed acceptable targets
the run is marked as failed due to p95 latency greater than <p95_ms> ms
or throughput less than <rps> requests per second
or error rate greater than <error_rate_percent> percent

US-143.name: US-143 Data Growth Scalability
US-143.description:
As a QA Engineer
I want to Validate storage and indexing scalability under increasing message and media volumes without performance degradation
So that Ensures the system can handle data growth while maintaining consistent performance and reliability

background.description: a baseline dataset and baseline performance metrics are available
the load test environment is provisioned with monitoring enabled

scenarios.name: Happy path: P95 indexing latency and query response meet targets at 10x data volume
scenarios.description: Validates indexing and query performance under 10x baseline data growth meets acceptance targets

steps.description: synthetic data growth to 10x baseline volume is loaded
indexing latency and query response times are measured for benchmark queries
P95 indexing latency increase is <= 20% versus baseline
P95 query response time is <= 1.2x baseline

scenarios.name: Boundary: storage growth remains linear within ±10% at incremental loads
scenarios.description: Validates storage utilization grows linearly at boundary thresholds of expected growth

steps.description: incremental data loads are applied at <dataset_scale> of baseline
storage utilization is measured against the expected linear growth model
storage utilization is within <tolerance> of expected growth

scenarios.name: Edge case: sustained ingestion throughput at high volume remains >= 90% baseline
scenarios.description: Validates ingestion throughput under continuous loading at scaled volumes stays within threshold

steps.description: continuous data loading runs at <dataset_scale> baseline volume
ingestion throughput is measured over a sustained window
sustained ingestion throughput is >= <throughput_threshold> of baseline

scenarios.name: Error scenario: performance targets are violated due to indexing bottleneck
scenarios.description: Validates system reports failure when indexing latency exceeds target thresholds

steps.description: synthetic data growth to 10x baseline volume is loaded with an induced indexing bottleneck
indexing latency is measured for the dataset
P95 indexing latency increase is > 20% versus baseline
the performance test run is marked as failed with a bottleneck alert

US-144.name: Service uptime SLA verification
US-144.description:
As a DevOps Engineer
I want to verify service uptime meets SLA targets for messaging, authentication, and group services
So that ensure high availability and compliance with business continuity expectations

background.description: monitoring data and incident logs are available for the selected calendar month

scenarios.name: Monthly uptime meets SLA for all services
scenarios.description: Validates monthly uptime is at or above 99.9% for each service

steps.description: the system is analyzing monitoring data for the calendar month
monthly uptime is calculated for the <service>
the uptime result is >= 99.9%
the calculated uptime is stored in the SLA report

scenarios.name: Uptime at SLA boundary
scenarios.description: Ensures 99.9% uptime exactly is accepted as compliant

steps.description: the system is analyzing monitoring data for the calendar month
monthly uptime is calculated for the <service> with value 99.9%
the uptime result is marked as compliant

scenarios.name: Uptime slightly below SLA target
scenarios.description: Validates a failure when uptime is below 99.9%

steps.description: the system is analyzing monitoring data for the calendar month
monthly uptime is calculated for the <service> with value 99.89%
the uptime result is marked as non-compliant
an SLA breach is recorded for the service

scenarios.name: MTTR meets SLA target
scenarios.description: Validates mean time to recover for critical incidents is <= 60 minutes

steps.description: incident response records are available for the period
MTTR is calculated for critical incidents
the MTTR result is <= 60 minutes

scenarios.name: MTTR exceeds SLA target
scenarios.description: Validates a failure when MTTR is above 60 minutes

steps.description: incident response records are available for the period
MTTR is calculated for critical incidents with value 60.1 minutes
the MTTR result is marked as non-compliant
an MTTR breach is recorded in the SLA report

scenarios.name: Missing monitoring data for month
scenarios.description: Handles error when monitoring data is incomplete or unavailable

steps.description: monitoring data for the calendar month is missing or incomplete
monthly uptime is requested for the messaging service
the system returns a data availability error
no SLA compliance status is generated

US-145.name: Verify failover time and user impact during node and zone failures
US-145.description:
As a DevOps Engineer
I want to Verify automatic failover time and user impact during node and zone failures
So that Ensures service continuity and minimal disruption to users during infrastructure failures

background.description: staging environment is configured with synthetic monitoring, APM, and load testing instrumentation enabled

scenarios.name: Failover time meets target for node failure
scenarios.description: Validates node failure failover time within 60 seconds under controlled conditions

steps.description: a controlled node failure is induced on a single node in the cluster
synthetic monitoring measures service availability restoration time
the failover time is less than or equal to 60 seconds
service availability is restored for all critical endpoints

scenarios.name: Failover time meets target for zone outage
scenarios.description: Validates zone failover routing within 120 seconds using monitoring timestamps

steps.description: a zone outage is simulated for the primary availability zone
monitoring timestamps capture traffic routing to the healthy zone
the failover time is less than or equal to 120 seconds
all incoming traffic is served by the healthy zone

scenarios.name: User-visible error rate remains within target during failover
scenarios.description: Validates error rate does not exceed 1% during the 5-minute failover window

steps.description: failover drills are running with APM error capture and load testing enabled
the user-visible HTTP error rate is measured over a 5-minute window
the error rate is less than or equal to 1% of requests
no critical endpoint exceeds the error rate threshold

scenarios.name: Session continuity rate meets threshold during failover
scenarios.description: Validates session retention across failover using session logs and synthetic journeys

steps.description: active user sessions are established before the failover drill
session logs and synthetic user journeys are analyzed during failover
the session continuity rate is greater than or equal to 99%
session drops are correlated to failover timestamps

scenarios.name: Failover time boundary conditions for node and zone failures
scenarios.description: Validates boundary values at exact thresholds for node and zone failover times

steps.description: a <failure_type> is induced under controlled conditions
the failover time is measured as <measured_seconds> seconds
the result is <expected_outcome> against the <threshold_seconds> seconds threshold

scenarios.name: Failover exceeds target thresholds
scenarios.description: Validates system behavior when failover time exceeds targets

steps.description: a <failure_type> is induced with constrained recovery conditions
the failover time is measured as <measured_seconds> seconds
the result is flagged as a failure against the <threshold_seconds> seconds target
an incident alert is generated for the exceeded threshold

scenarios.name: Session continuity drops below threshold
scenarios.description: Validates error scenario when session continuity is below target

steps.description: active user sessions are established before the failover drill
session continuity is measured as <continuity_rate> percent during failover
the result is flagged as a failure against the 99% target
a session retention report is generated with impacted sessions

US-146.name: Verify transport and at-rest encryption across production systems
US-146.description:
As a Security Team
I want to Verify that all data in transit and at rest is encrypted using modern standards across production systems
So that Ensures confidentiality and compliance by preventing data exposure during transmission or storage

background.description: production inventory and configuration baselines are available for endpoints, services, and storage

scenarios.name: Validate transport encryption meets TLS 1.2+ with strong ciphers
scenarios.description: Happy path verification for transport encryption across external and internal endpoints

steps.description: automated TLS scanners are configured for all external and internal endpoints
the system measures transport protocol and cipher suites for each endpoint
all endpoints report TLS 1.2 or higher
all endpoints negotiate strong ciphers such as AES-256-GCM or ChaCha20-Poly1305

scenarios.name: At-rest encryption coverage reaches 100% across storage types
scenarios.description: Happy path verification for full at-rest encryption coverage

steps.description: audit reports are available for databases, object storage, and block storage volumes
the system measures at-rest encryption coverage
all databases, object storage, and block storage volumes are encrypted with AES-256 or equivalent
no unencrypted production storage resources are detected

scenarios.name: Centralized key management with rotation within 12 months
scenarios.description: Happy path verification for key management, rotation, and access controls

steps.description: KMS/HSM policies, rotation schedules, and access logs are available
the system measures key management, rotation cadence, and access controls
keys are managed centrally by KMS or HSM
key rotation is scheduled at least every 12 months with enforced access controls

scenarios.name: Detect endpoints using deprecated TLS protocols or weak ciphers
scenarios.description: Error scenario for transport encryption non-compliance

steps.description: an endpoint inventory includes services with legacy configurations
the system measures transport protocol and cipher suites for each endpoint
endpoints using TLS 1.1 or lower are flagged as non-compliant
endpoints negotiating weak ciphers are reported with remediation guidance

scenarios.name: Identify unencrypted at-rest resources during audits
scenarios.description: Error scenario for at-rest encryption gaps

steps.description: audit reports include newly provisioned storage resources
the system measures at-rest encryption coverage
any unencrypted database, object storage, or block storage volume is flagged
the report indicates failure to meet 100% coverage target

scenarios.name: Key rotation overdue beyond 12 months
scenarios.description: Boundary condition for key rotation schedule

steps.description: KMS/HSM rotation schedule records include last-rotation timestamps
the system evaluates key rotation intervals
keys last rotated more than 12 months ago are marked non-compliant
rotation exceptions are reported with owners and required actions

scenarios.name: Transport encryption compliance by endpoint category
scenarios.description: Edge case validation by endpoint type with mixed configurations

steps.description: endpoint categories include external, internal, and service-mesh endpoints
the system measures protocol and cipher compliance per category
each category meets TLS 1.2+ and strong cipher requirements
any category-level deviation is reported separately

scenarios.name: At-rest encryption algorithm boundary acceptance
scenarios.description: Boundary condition for accepted algorithms at rest

steps.description: audit reports list storage encryption algorithms
the system validates algorithms against the policy baseline
AES-256 is accepted as compliant and AES-128 is rejected
equivalent-strength algorithms are marked compliant

scenarios.name: Transport encryption compliance by protocol and cipher
scenarios.description: Data-driven validation of protocol and cipher combinations

steps.description: a set of endpoints with varying protocol and cipher configurations
the system measures transport protocol and cipher suites
the endpoint compliance result matches the expected outcome

scenarios.name: At-rest encryption compliance by storage type
scenarios.description: Data-driven validation of encryption coverage by storage type

steps.description: storage audit entries include encryption status and algorithm
the system evaluates at-rest encryption for each storage item
each storage item is marked compliant based on encryption status and algorithm

scenarios.name: Key management compliance by rotation interval
scenarios.description: Data-driven validation of key rotation intervals and control enforcement

steps.description: KMS/HSM key records include last rotation date and access control status
the system checks rotation interval and access control enforcement
the key record compliance result matches the expected outcome

US-147.name: Authentication hardening verification
US-147.description:
As a Security Team
I want to verify authentication controls and audit logging meet hardening standards
So that ensures strong protection against unauthorized access and provides traceable audit evidence

background.description: the authentication policy and audit logging configuration are accessible for review

scenarios.name: MFA enforcement meets targets for privileged and standard accounts
scenarios.description: Validates MFA enforcement rates meet required thresholds

steps.description: privileged and standard user accounts are configured with MFA
I measure the MFA enforcement rate across recent login events
privileged account logins show 100% MFA enforcement
standard account logins show at least 95% MFA enforcement

scenarios.name: Account lockout triggers after 5 failures within 10 minutes
scenarios.description: Validates lockout behavior at the required boundary

steps.description: a standard user account is active and not locked
I submit 5 consecutive failed login attempts within 10 minutes
the account is locked immediately after the 5th failed attempt
subsequent login attempts are rejected due to lockout

scenarios.name: Account does not lock for 4 failures within 10 minutes
scenarios.description: Validates edge case below the lockout threshold

steps.description: a standard user account is active and not locked
I submit 4 consecutive failed login attempts within 10 minutes
the account remains unlocked
a subsequent valid login is allowed

scenarios.name: Authentication audit logs include required fields for all event types
scenarios.description: Verifies audit log completeness for login, logout, failure, and password change events

steps.description: test events for login, logout, failure, and password change are executed
I inspect the corresponding audit log entries
each event is logged with user, timestamp, source IP, and outcome
no event type is missing from the audit logs

scenarios.name: Audit log retention meets minimum duration and tamper-evident storage
scenarios.description: Validates retention period and tamper-evident storage configuration

steps.description: audit log storage configuration and retention settings are available
I verify the configured retention period and storage mode
retention is configured for at least 365 days
storage is tamper-evident

scenarios.name: MFA enforcement below threshold is flagged
scenarios.description: Validates error handling when enforcement rates do not meet targets

steps.description: MFA enforcement metrics are collected for privileged and standard users
the privileged MFA rate is 99% or the standard MFA rate is below 95%
the control is reported as non-compliant
a remediation action is required

scenarios.name: Lockout does not trigger when failures are outside 10-minute window
scenarios.description: Validates boundary condition for time window enforcement

steps.description: a standard user account is active and not locked
I submit 5 failed login attempts spread over more than 10 minutes
the account remains unlocked
a valid login succeeds

scenarios.name: Missing audit log fields are flagged as non-compliant
scenarios.description: Validates error scenario when audit log entries are incomplete

steps.description: authentication events have been executed
I find an audit log entry missing user, timestamp, source IP, or outcome
the audit logging control is reported as non-compliant
the missing field is recorded for remediation

scenarios.name: Data-driven MFA enforcement rate validation
scenarios.description: Validates enforcement rate thresholds using multiple data sets

steps.description: MFA enforcement metrics are available for the evaluated period
I evaluate privileged MFA rate <privileged_rate> and standard MFA rate <standard_rate>
the compliance result is <expected_result>
the evaluation is recorded with the measured rates

scenarios.name: Data-driven lockout boundary validation
scenarios.description: Validates lockout behavior using multiple failure counts and windows

steps.description: a standard user account is active and not locked
I submit <failure_count> failed login attempts within <window_minutes> minutes
the lockout status is <expected_status>
the outcome is recorded in audit logs

US-148.name: Verify data minimization and retention compliance
US-148.description:
As a QA Engineer
I want to Verify that personal data is retained only for defined periods and is deletable upon request
So that Ensures compliance with data minimization principles and user privacy obligations

background.description: retention schedules and deletion policies are configured and active

scenarios.name: Retention duration compliance meets 100% target
scenarios.description: Happy path verifying all personal data records are within approved retention periods

steps.description: the system is under Review retention policy mappings and run database queries to compare record timestamps against retention schedules conditions
measuring Retention duration compliance
the result meets target: 100% of personal data records stored no longer than the approved retention period
all retention schedule mappings are found for each personal data category

scenarios.name: Deletion request fulfillment time meets SLA thresholds
scenarios.description: Happy path verifying deletion requests are completed within policy timelines

steps.description: the system is under Audit deletion request logs and timestamps from intake to completion conditions
measuring Deletion request fulfillment time
the result meets target: 95% of deletion requests completed within 30 days; 100% within 45 days
no request has a negative or missing completion timestamp

scenarios.name: Deletion completeness across primary and backups
scenarios.description: Happy path verifying deleted data is removed from primary and backup systems

steps.description: the system is under Perform data discovery scans and backup restoration tests to confirm absence of deleted records conditions
measuring Deletion completeness
the result meets target: 100% of requested personal data removed from primary and backup systems per policy
no deleted identifiers are detected in discovery scan results

scenarios.name: Retention boundary conditions by category
scenarios.description: Boundary testing of records at, just before, and just after retention limits

steps.description: retention schedules specify <retention_days> days for <category>
a record for <category> has an age of <record_age_days> days
measuring Retention duration compliance
the record is <expected_status> retention compliance

scenarios.name: Deletion SLA edge cases around 30 and 45 day limits
scenarios.description: Edge case validation for deletion fulfillment thresholds

steps.description: a deletion request is completed in <completion_days> days
measuring Deletion request fulfillment time
the request is <expected_sla_status> the SLA policy

scenarios.name: Missing retention mapping triggers error handling
scenarios.description: Error scenario when a personal data category lacks a retention schedule mapping

steps.description: a personal data category exists without a retention schedule mapping
measuring Retention duration compliance
the system flags a compliance error for missing retention mapping
the compliance report excludes the unmapped category from the 100% calculation

scenarios.name: Deletion completeness error when backups retain records
scenarios.description: Error scenario when deleted records are still present in backups

steps.description: a deletion request has been completed for a user
backup restoration tests reveal residual records for that user
measuring Deletion completeness
the system reports a deletion completeness failure
the residual records are listed for remediation

US-149.name: Compliance reporting verification for GDPR and CCPA
US-149.description:
As a Security Team
I want to verify that privacy controls and compliance reporting meet GDPR and CCPA requirements
So that to ensure regulatory compliance and reduce legal and reputational risk

background.description: the compliance reporting system is available in the test environment

scenarios.name: GDPR reporting coverage is 100% for required elements
scenarios.description: Validates full GDPR reporting coverage for all required elements

steps.description: the GDPR checklist contains all required reporting elements
I measure GDPR compliance coverage in the generated report
the coverage result is 100% of required GDPR reporting elements present
the report lists data processing, consent, data subject rights, and breach notification elements

scenarios.name: CCPA reporting coverage is 100% for required elements
scenarios.description: Validates full CCPA reporting coverage for all required elements

steps.description: the CCPA checklist contains all required reporting elements
I measure CCPA compliance coverage in the generated report
the coverage result is 100% of required CCPA reporting elements present
the report lists notice, opt-out, access, deletion, and non-discrimination elements

scenarios.name: Evidence traceability is complete for sampled report items
scenarios.description: Ensures evidence artifacts and logs are linked for sampled controls

steps.description: a 30% random sample of report items is selected
I verify evidence artifacts and system logs for sampled items
100% of sampled reported controls are linked to auditable evidence
each evidence link is accessible and time-stamped

scenarios.name: Report generation is available on demand within 24 hours
scenarios.description: Validates report generation availability and timeliness

steps.description: a compliance report request is submitted
I measure the time until the report is available
the report is available on demand
the report becomes available within 24 hours of the request

scenarios.name: Scenario Outline: GDPR coverage edge case with minimum required elements
scenarios.description: Checks boundary coverage when only minimum required elements are present

steps.description: the report includes exactly the minimum GDPR elements: <elements>
I measure GDPR compliance coverage in the generated report
the coverage result is <expected_coverage>

examples.elements: data processing, consent, data subject rights, breach notification

scenarios.name: Scenario Outline: CCPA coverage error when a required element is missing
scenarios.description: Verifies failure when any required CCPA reporting element is missing

steps.description: the report is missing the required CCPA element: <missing_element>
I measure CCPA compliance coverage in the generated report
the coverage result is less than 100%
the missing element <missing_element> is flagged in the coverage report

scenarios.name: Scenario Outline: Report availability boundary condition at 24 hours
scenarios.description: Validates boundary timing for report availability

steps.description: a compliance report request is submitted at <request_time>
the report becomes available at <available_time>
the availability time is within 24 hours

scenarios.name: Scenario Outline: Evidence traceability error when evidence link is missing
scenarios.description: Ensures missing evidence links are detected for sampled items

steps.description: a 30% random sample of report items is selected
I verify evidence artifacts and system logs for sampled items
any item with missing evidence links is reported as non-compliant
the missing evidence reference for <control_id> is logged

US-150.name: Message delivery reliability under intermittent network conditions
US-150.description:
As a QA Engineer
I want to verify reliable message delivery under intermittent network conditions
So that ensures users receive messages consistently despite network instability

background.description: a controlled test environment with network drop and recovery simulation is available
message sender and receiver logs are enabled with synchronized timestamps

scenarios.name: Successful delivery rate meets target after network restoration
scenarios.description: Validates that the delivery success rate is at or above the target during intermittent connectivity

steps.description: the network is configured to drop for 30 seconds and recover
10,000 messages are queued during the drop window
delivery outcomes are measured within 5 minutes after network restoration
the delivery success rate is at least 99.5%
all delivery timestamps are within the 5 minute window

scenarios.name: Edge case: minimal intermittent drop with small batch still meets delivery target
scenarios.description: Validates delivery success rate for a small batch during brief drops

steps.description: the network is configured to drop for 5 seconds and recover
100 messages are queued during the drop window
delivery outcomes are measured within 5 minutes after network restoration
the delivery success rate is at least 99.5%
no message delivery exceeds the 5 minute recovery window

scenarios.name: Boundary condition: delivery rate at exactly 99.5%
scenarios.description: Confirms acceptance when delivery success rate is exactly at the threshold

steps.description: the network is configured to drop for 30 seconds and recover
10,000 messages are queued during the drop window
delivery outcomes show 9,950 delivered within 5 minutes after restoration
the delivery success rate meets the 99.5% target

scenarios.name: Message loss rate within acceptable threshold
scenarios.description: Validates that message loss rate is at or below the target across 10,000 messages

steps.description: intermittent network drops occur during message transmission
a fixed set of 10,000 messages is sent
sender and receiver logs are reconciled
the message loss rate is at most 0.1%

scenarios.name: Boundary condition: loss rate at exactly 0.1%
scenarios.description: Confirms acceptance when loss rate is exactly at the threshold

steps.description: a fixed set of 10,000 messages is sent during intermittent connectivity
log reconciliation shows 10 messages missing
the message loss rate meets the 0.1% target

scenarios.name: Duplicate message rate within acceptable threshold
scenarios.description: Validates that duplicate messages are within the allowed rate

steps.description: intermittent network disruptions occur during message delivery
receiver logs are analyzed for duplicate message IDs
the duplicate message rate is at most 0.05%

scenarios.name: Boundary condition: duplicate rate at exactly 0.05%
scenarios.description: Confirms acceptance when duplicate rate is exactly at the threshold

steps.description: 10,000 messages are delivered after a network disruption
receiver logs show 5 duplicate message IDs
the duplicate message rate meets the 0.05% target

scenarios.name: Error scenario: delivery success rate below target
scenarios.description: Validates failure behavior when delivery success rate is below 99.5%

steps.description: the network is configured to drop for 30 seconds and recover
10,000 messages are queued during the drop window
delivery outcomes show 9,900 delivered within 5 minutes after restoration
the delivery success rate fails the 99.5% target

scenarios.name: Error scenario: loss rate above target
scenarios.description: Validates failure behavior when message loss exceeds 0.1%

steps.description: a fixed set of 10,000 messages is sent during intermittent connectivity
log reconciliation shows 25 messages missing
the message loss rate fails the 0.1% target

scenarios.name: Error scenario: duplicate rate above target
scenarios.description: Validates failure behavior when duplicate messages exceed 0.05%

steps.description: 10,000 messages are delivered after a network disruption
receiver logs show 12 duplicate message IDs
the duplicate message rate fails the 0.05% target

scenarios.name: Scenario Outline: Delivery success rate under varying drop durations
scenarios.description: Data-driven validation of delivery success rate across multiple drop durations

steps.description: the network is configured to drop for <drop_duration_seconds> seconds and recover
10,000 messages are queued during the drop window
delivery outcomes are measured within 5 minutes after network restoration
the delivery success rate is at least <expected_success_rate>%

scenarios.name: Scenario Outline: Loss and duplicate rates across batch sizes
scenarios.description: Data-driven validation of loss and duplicate rates across different batch sizes

steps.description: intermittent network drops occur during message transmission
<batch_size> messages are sent
sender and receiver logs are reconciled and duplicate IDs are counted
the loss rate is at most <max_loss_rate>%
the duplicate rate is at most <max_duplicate_rate>%

US-151.name: Verify Error Budget Definitions and Enforcement
US-151.description:
As a DevOps Engineer
I want to verify that reliability targets are defined and enforced through explicit error budgets with monitoring and alerting in place
So that ensures reliability expectations are measurable and enforced, preventing unbounded risk to service availability

background.description: the service catalog, SLO documentation, and monitoring data sources are available

scenarios.name: Happy path: 100% of critical services have explicit error budgets aligned to SLOs
scenarios.description: Validates policy coverage for critical services with documented error budgets

steps.description: the critical services list and SLO documents are loaded
the system measures error budget policy coverage
the coverage result equals 100%
each critical service has an explicit error budget aligned to its SLO

scenarios.name: Boundary: Error budget calculation accuracy within ±1% variance
scenarios.description: Validates recalculated error budgets match reported values at the accuracy boundary

steps.description: monitoring data and reported error budget values exist for each service
the system recalculates error budgets from SLOs and actual uptime
the variance between recalculated and reported values is within the allowed threshold
the variance equals the expected boundary value

scenarios.name: Edge case: Burn rate breach triggers alert at threshold
scenarios.description: Validates enforcement when burn rate is exactly at threshold for the defined window

steps.description: a burn rate threshold and alerting rule are defined
monitoring simulates a burn rate at the threshold for the specified duration
an alert is triggered
the alert contains the service name and breach window

scenarios.name: Error scenario: Missing error budget definition for a critical service
scenarios.description: Validates detection of missing error budget definitions

steps.description: a critical service exists without an error budget definition
the system measures error budget policy coverage
the coverage result is less than 100%
the missing service is reported in the coverage gap list

scenarios.name: Error scenario: Burn rate below threshold does not trigger alert
scenarios.description: Validates no alert is fired when burn rate is below the defined threshold

steps.description: a burn rate threshold and alerting rule are defined
monitoring simulates a burn rate below the threshold for the specified duration
no alert is triggered
the burn rate is recorded without enforcement actions

US-152.name: Enforce code quality gates and coverage thresholds in CI/CD
US-152.description:
As a QA Engineer
I want to Verify that code quality gates and automated test coverage thresholds are enforced in CI/CD
So that Ensures maintainable code and reduces defects through consistent automated testing standards

background.description: the CI pipeline is configured with coverage thresholds and quality gate rules

scenarios.name: Happy path: All quality checks pass on main branch
scenarios.description: Validates that coverage, quality gate, and tests pass under normal conditions

steps.description: the main branch build is triggered with the full test suite
the CI pipeline generates the coverage report and runs static analysis
unit test line coverage on core modules is at least 80%
the quality gate status is Pass with no blocker or critical issues
all automated tests pass and the build is green

scenarios.name: Boundary: Coverage exactly at the minimum threshold
scenarios.description: Ensures the pipeline accepts coverage at the boundary value

steps.description: the coverage report is generated for core modules
the measured line coverage is exactly 80%
the coverage gate is marked as passed
the pipeline proceeds to the next stage

scenarios.name: Edge case: Coverage slightly above threshold with minor issues
scenarios.description: Validates that passing coverage does not override quality gate failures

steps.description: core module coverage is 80.1% and static analysis is executed
static analysis reports one critical issue
the quality gate status is Fail
the pipeline fails despite coverage meeting the threshold

scenarios.name: Error scenario: Coverage below threshold fails the gate
scenarios.description: Verifies pipeline failure when coverage is below minimum

steps.description: the coverage report is generated for core modules
the measured line coverage is below 80%
the coverage gate is marked as failed
the pipeline status is failed

scenarios.name: Scenario Outline: Automated test execution results on main branch
scenarios.description: Data-driven validation of test pass rates affecting build status

steps.description: the main branch build runs the full automated test suite
the test pass rate is <pass_rate>%
the build status is <build_status>
the pipeline enforces the 100% pass requirement

scenarios.name: Scenario Outline: Static analysis quality gate outcomes
scenarios.description: Validates quality gate logic for blocker and critical issues

steps.description: the CI pipeline runs static analysis with configured rules
the analysis reports <blocker_count> blocker issues and <critical_count> critical issues
the quality gate status is <gate_status>
the pipeline enforces the quality gate decision

US-153.name: Deployment Frequency and Reliability Metrics Verification
US-153.description:
As a DevOps Engineer
I want to Verify that the deployment pipeline supports frequent, low-risk releases through automation, rollback capability, and short lead times
So that Ensures the system can deliver changes rapidly with minimal disruption, reducing risk and improving responsiveness to stakeholder needs

background.description: the 4-week review period is defined with complete CI/CD, incident, and audit logs

scenarios.name: Happy path: All deployment performance targets are met
scenarios.description: Validates that frequency, change failure rate, lead time, and automation coverage meet targets

steps.description: the deployment logs show 4 weeks of production deployments
the system calculates deployment frequency, change failure rate, lead time, and automation coverage
deployment frequency is at least 3 per week
change failure rate is less than or equal to 5 percent
median lead time from merge to production is under 24 hours
automation coverage for production deployments is 100 percent

scenarios.name: Boundary conditions: Metrics exactly at thresholds
scenarios.description: Ensures thresholds are inclusive at boundary values

steps.description: the 4-week period has <total_deployments> production deployments and <rollbacks> rollbacks or hotfixes
median lead time from merge to production is <median_lead_time_hours> hours
all production deployments were executed by the CI/CD pipeline
the system evaluates deployment metrics
deployment frequency meets the target of at least 3 per week
change failure rate meets the target of less than or equal to 5 percent
median lead time meets the target under 24 hours

scenarios.name: Edge case: Deployment frequency just below target
scenarios.description: Validates failure when deployment frequency is below minimum

steps.description: the 4-week period has <total_deployments> production deployments
deployment frequency is calculated
the deployment frequency target is not met

scenarios.name: Edge case: Change failure rate exceeds target
scenarios.description: Validates failure when rollbacks or hotfixes exceed 5 percent

steps.description: the 4-week period has <total_deployments> production deployments and <rollbacks> rollbacks or hotfixes
change failure rate is calculated
the change failure rate target is not met

scenarios.name: Error scenario: Missing logs prevent metric calculation
scenarios.description: Ensures an error is reported when required logs are unavailable

steps.description: CI/CD deployment logs for the 4-week period are missing
the system attempts to calculate deployment frequency
the system reports an error indicating missing deployment logs

scenarios.name: Edge case: Automated deployment coverage is below 100 percent
scenarios.description: Validates failure when any production deployment is not via CI/CD pipeline

steps.description: the 4-week period has <total_deployments> production deployments with <manual_deployments> manual executions
automated deployment coverage is calculated
the automated deployment coverage target is not met

US-154.name: Verify Centralized Logging
US-154.description:
As a DevOps Engineer
I want to verify that all services emit structured logs to the centralized logging pipeline
So that ensures consistent troubleshooting and efficient root cause analysis across services

background.description: the centralized logging pipeline is available and all services are deployed

scenarios.name: All services emit structured logs with required fields
scenarios.description: Validates 100% of services emit JSON logs with required fields

steps.description: audit log samples are collected from each service
the samples are validated for required fields: timestamp, level, service, correlation_id, message in JSON format
the percentage of services emitting structured logs is 100%
all samples conform to valid JSON syntax

scenarios.name: Log ingestion success rate meets target over 24 hours
scenarios.description: Compares emitted vs ingested log count across services to meet the target threshold

steps.description: each service has emitted logs over the last 24 hours
the emitted log count per service is compared with ingested log count
the log ingestion success rate is greater than or equal to 99.9%
all services are included in the comparison

scenarios.name: Correlation ID search retrieves log within SLA
scenarios.description: Verifies search retrieval time meets the <= 30 seconds target

steps.description: a known correlation_id exists in the centralized logging platform
a search query is executed using the correlation_id
the log entry is retrieved within 30 seconds
the retrieved entry matches the requested correlation_id

scenarios.name: Boundary: ingestion success rate at exact threshold
scenarios.description: Validates acceptance when ingestion success rate equals 99.9%

steps.description: emitted and ingested log counts are collected for the last 24 hours
the ingestion success rate is calculated
the result equals 99.9% and passes the target
the calculation uses the same service set for emitted and ingested logs

scenarios.name: Edge case: single service emits logs with all required fields
scenarios.description: Ensures validation logic works with minimal service count

steps.description: audit log samples are collected from a single service
the sample is validated for required fields in JSON format
the percentage of services emitting structured logs is 100%
the sample includes timestamp, level, service, correlation_id, and message

scenarios.name: Error: service emits log missing required field
scenarios.description: Detects non-compliant log structure

steps.description: audit log samples include a service emitting JSON logs missing the correlation_id field
the samples are validated for required fields
the percentage of services emitting structured logs is less than 100%
the validation report flags the missing field

scenarios.name: Error: correlation ID search exceeds SLA
scenarios.description: Fails when retrieval time is greater than 30 seconds

steps.description: a known correlation_id exists in the centralized logging platform
a search query is executed using the correlation_id
the retrieval time is greater than 30 seconds and the SLA is not met
the monitoring alert is raised for slow retrieval

US-155.name: Verify Metrics and Alerting
US-155.description:
As a DevOps Engineer
I want to verify that key service metrics and alerting enable rapid incident detection
So that ensures operational issues are detected quickly to minimize impact and downtime

background.description: the monitoring system and alerting rules are configured for all defined critical services

scenarios.name: Alert is generated within target time for a critical incident
scenarios.description: Validates alert creation time meets the 2-minute target under controlled fault

steps.description: a controlled fault is triggered on a critical service to breach a critical threshold
the time from threshold breach to alert creation is measured
the alert is created within 2 minutes
the alert is classified as critical for the affected service

scenarios.name: All critical services emit required key metrics
scenarios.description: Validates 100% metric coverage for critical services

steps.description: the list of defined critical services is available
metric ingestion is audited for each critical service
CPU, memory, latency, error rate, and availability metrics are present for all critical services

scenarios.name: False positive rate remains below threshold over 30 days
scenarios.description: Validates alert accuracy against incident tickets and alert history

steps.description: alert history and incident tickets for the last 30 days are available
alerts are correlated with incident tickets
the false positive rate is below 5%

scenarios.name: Alert creation time at boundary condition of 2 minutes
scenarios.description: Validates boundary timing exactly at the target threshold

steps.description: a controlled fault is triggered at the exact critical threshold
the alert creation time is measured
the alert is created exactly at 2 minutes and is considered compliant

scenarios.name: Metric ingestion delay causes missing metric for one service
scenarios.description: Validates edge case where ingestion lag causes temporary metric gaps

steps.description: metric ingestion latency spikes for a single critical service
metric coverage is measured during the ingestion delay
the coverage check reports missing metrics for that service
the overall coverage result is marked as non-compliant

scenarios.name: Alert is not generated within target time
scenarios.description: Validates error scenario when alert creation exceeds target

steps.description: a controlled fault breaches a critical threshold
the alert creation time is measured
the alert creation time exceeds 2 minutes
the result is flagged as a failure

scenarios.name: False positive rate exceeds 5 percent
scenarios.description: Validates error scenario when alert accuracy target is not met

steps.description: alert history and incident tickets for the last 30 days are available
alerts are correlated with incident tickets
the false positive rate is 5 percent or higher
the alert accuracy check fails

US-156.name: WCAG 2.1 AA Compliance Verification
US-156.description:
As a QA Engineer
I want to validate that all user interfaces meet WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility standards
So that ensures users with disabilities can effectively use the system and reduces compliance risk

background.description: the WCAG 2.1 AA checklist, automated scanner, and test environments are available

scenarios.name: Full WCAG conformance on core pages
scenarios.description: Validates 100% WCAG 2.1 AA success criteria pass on tested pages

steps.description: automated accessibility scans and manual audits are configured for all core pages
the WCAG 2.1 AA conformance results are measured
100% of tested pages pass all WCAG 2.1 AA success criteria
all findings are recorded with no critical or major violations

scenarios.name: Keyboard-only navigation across critical flows
scenarios.description: Ensures all interactive elements are reachable and operable via keyboard

steps.description: manual keyboard-only navigation tests are prepared for critical user flows
each flow is completed using only Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter, and Space
100% of interactive elements are reachable and operable via keyboard
focus indicators remain visible on all focusable elements

scenarios.name: Screen reader usability on supported platforms
scenarios.description: Validates critical flows are fully usable with NVDA and VoiceOver

steps.description: manual tests using NVDA on Windows and VoiceOver on macOS are prepared
critical user flows are executed with a screen reader enabled
all critical flows are fully usable with the screen reader
all form fields and controls have accurate accessible names

scenarios.name: Color contrast compliance across text sizes
scenarios.description: Verifies contrast ratios meet WCAG 2.1 AA thresholds

steps.description: automated contrast checks are configured with spot manual verification
contrast ratios are measured for normal and large text
normal text meets 4.5:1 and large text meets 3:1 minimum contrast ratios
UI components and graphical objects meet the minimum required contrast

scenarios.name: Outline - WCAG scan results by page set
scenarios.description: Data-driven validation of WCAG conformance by page group

steps.description: the page set <page_set> is selected for automated scanning and manual audit
WCAG 2.1 AA conformance is measured
the pass rate is <expected_pass_rate> for the selected page set
all high-impact issues are captured in the report

scenarios.name: Outline - Keyboard navigation on dynamic components
scenarios.description: Validates keyboard accessibility on dynamic UI elements

steps.description: the dynamic component <component> is available in a core flow
the component is operated using only the keyboard
all actions are possible and focus order is logical
no keyboard trap occurs

scenarios.name: Outline - Color contrast boundary values
scenarios.description: Checks boundary contrast ratios at thresholds

steps.description: a text element of size <text_size> is rendered
the measured contrast ratio is <contrast_ratio>
the contrast evaluation result is <expected_result>

scenarios.name: Accessibility scan detects violations
scenarios.description: Ensures failures are reported when WCAG criteria are not met

steps.description: a page contains missing form labels and insufficient contrast
automated and manual WCAG 2.1 AA checks are executed
the page fails WCAG 2.1 AA conformance
violations are logged with severity and remediation guidance

scenarios.name: Screen reader fails on critical flow
scenarios.description: Validates that critical flow usability failures are detected

steps.description: a critical flow includes a button without an accessible name
the flow is executed with a screen reader enabled
the flow is not fully usable and is marked as failed
the missing accessible name is recorded as a blocking issue

scenarios.name: Keyboard navigation blocked by a focus trap
scenarios.description: Ensures keyboard traps are detected and reported

steps.description: a modal dialog opens without a focus escape path
the user attempts to navigate out using keyboard only
a keyboard trap is detected and the test fails
the failure is logged with reproduction steps

US-157.name: Assistive technology support for core user flows
US-157.description:
As a QA Engineer
I want to Verify that core user flows are fully operable with screen readers and keyboard-only navigation
So that Ensures accessibility compliance and usability for users relying on assistive technologies

background.description: the test environment is configured with NVDA on Windows and VoiceOver on macOS

scenarios.name: Happy path: Screen reader labels, roles, and focus order are complete for all core flow screens
scenarios.description: Validates 100% screen reader coverage for meaningful labels, roles, and focus order in core flows

steps.description: the accessibility tree inspection tool is enabled
the tester runs the scripted walkthrough for all core flow screens
each screen exposes meaningful labels and roles to the screen reader
the focus order announced by the screen reader matches the visual flow

scenarios.name: Scenario Outline: Keyboard-only task completion across core flows
scenarios.description: Ensures tasks can be completed using only Tab/Shift+Tab/Enter/Space

steps.description: the user starts at the <start_page> of the <core_flow>
the user completes the flow using only keyboard navigation
the <core_flow> is completed without using a mouse
all required fields are reachable and actionable via keyboard

scenarios.name: Edge case: Focus visibility and order on dynamic content updates
scenarios.description: Checks focus visibility and logical order when content is dynamically loaded

steps.description: a core flow page loads additional fields dynamically after a selection
the user tabs through interactive elements after the update
a visible focus indicator is present on each interactive element
the focus order remains logical and consistent with the visual layout

scenarios.name: Error scenario: Missing label or role on an interactive element
scenarios.description: Validates that missing accessible labels/roles are detected and reported

steps.description: an interactive element in a core flow lacks an accessible label or role
the screen reader inspects the accessibility tree for that element
the coverage check fails for that screen
the defect is logged with the element identifier and page context

scenarios.name: Boundary condition: Minimum focus indicator contrast and visibility
scenarios.description: Ensures focus indicators are visible at the minimum acceptable contrast threshold

steps.description: the UI theme is set to the lowest supported contrast setting
the user tabs through all interactive elements in a core flow page
the focus indicator is still visible on every element
the focus order remains logical and complete

US-158.name: Verify API standards compliance
US-158.description:
As a QA Engineer
I want to verify that all external and internal APIs conform to documented integration standards
So that ensures consistent integration behavior, reduces integration defects, and simplifies onboarding for consumers

background.description: all published API specifications and versions are available in the repository

scenarios.name: 100% compliance for linting and schema validation
scenarios.description: Happy path where all APIs conform to approved standard schema and style guide

steps.description: automated linting and schema validation are enabled against the API standards checklist
the compliance rate is measured for all published APIs
the compliance rate is 100%
no linting or schema violations are reported

scenarios.name: Contract tests pass for each API version
scenarios.description: Happy path where contract tests pass for request/response formats and error handling

steps.description: contract tests are configured in CI for each API version
the contract test pass rate is measured
the pass rate is 100%
all request, response, and error schemas match the specifications

scenarios.name: Documentation completeness for required sections
scenarios.description: Happy path where documentation includes required sections for all APIs

steps.description: documentation audit uses a checklist and automated OpenAPI spec parsing
documentation completeness is measured
100% of APIs include required documentation sections
the sections include auth, endpoints, error codes, and versioning

scenarios.name: Edge case for mixed API versions and legacy endpoints
scenarios.description: Edge case where multiple versions and legacy endpoints are included in compliance checks

steps.description: the inventory includes current and legacy API versions
linting, contract testing, and documentation audits run across all versions
all versions meet the 100% compliance targets
legacy endpoints do not introduce schema or documentation gaps

scenarios.name: Boundary condition for minimum required documentation sections
scenarios.description: Boundary test where APIs include exactly the minimum required documentation sections

steps.description: an API documentation set includes only the minimum required sections
documentation completeness is evaluated
the completeness score is 100%
no missing-section violations are reported

scenarios.name: Error scenario for non-compliant schemas detected
scenarios.description: Negative test where schema violations cause compliance rate to drop below target

steps.description: an API specification has a schema violation against the standard checklist
the compliance rate is measured for all published APIs
the compliance rate is below 100%
the violation is reported with a failing checklist rule

scenarios.name: Scenario Outline: Contract test failures by error handling variation
scenarios.description: Negative test for different error format deviations in contract tests

steps.description: a contract test suite runs for API version <version>
the API returns error response with <error_issue>
the contract test fails for error handling
the failure is reported with the <error_issue> detail

scenarios.name: Scenario Outline: Documentation section omissions
scenarios.description: Negative test where required documentation sections are missing

steps.description: API documentation is parsed for <api_name>
the <section> section is missing
documentation completeness is below 100%
the missing <section> is reported

US-159.name: Device interoperability verification across major OS versions
US-159.description:
As a QA Engineer
I want to verify multi-device functionality across major OS versions
So that ensure consistent user experience and prevent device-specific failures in supported environments

background.description: the device lab is configured with iOS, Android, Windows, and macOS major versions from the published compatibility matrix

scenarios.name: Achieve required functional pass rate across all major OS versions
scenarios.description: Validates that critical and high-priority test cases meet or exceed the target pass rate per OS

steps.description: automated and manual test suites are executed for each major OS version
the functional test pass rate is calculated for critical and high-priority test cases
the pass rate is at least 95% for each major OS version
the results are recorded per OS version for audit

scenarios.name: Boundary: Pass rate equals minimum threshold
scenarios.description: Ensures the system accepts the exact minimum pass rate of 95% per OS version

steps.description: test execution is complete for each major OS version
the pass rate for critical and high-priority test cases is computed
a pass rate of exactly 95% is treated as meeting the target
no compliance alerts are raised for the OS version

scenarios.name: Fail when pass rate is below target
scenarios.description: Validates error handling when a major OS version does not meet the 95% threshold

steps.description: automated and manual test suites are executed for a major OS version
the functional test pass rate is below 95%
the verification result is marked as failed for that OS version
a remediation action is required before release

scenarios.name: Complete device coverage against compatibility matrix
scenarios.description: Ensures 100% coverage of major OS versions and top 5 devices per OS by usage

steps.description: the test plan is mapped to the published compatibility matrix
executed devices and OS versions are recorded
coverage includes all major OS versions
coverage includes the top 5 devices per OS by usage

scenarios.name: Error when device coverage is incomplete
scenarios.description: Validates failure when any major OS version or top 5 devices are missing from execution

steps.description: the compatibility matrix is published for the release
executed device coverage is less than required
the coverage result is marked as non-compliant
the missing OS versions or devices are reported

scenarios.name: Boundary: Cross-device defect rate at maximum allowed
scenarios.description: Ensures that up to 2 critical device-specific defects per release is acceptable

steps.description: device/OS-specific defects are tracked in the issue tracker during the verification cycle
the count of critical device-specific defects is measured
a count of 2 or fewer is considered within target
the release is not blocked by defect rate

scenarios.name: Fail when cross-device defect rate exceeds target
scenarios.description: Validates error handling when critical device-specific defects exceed 2 per release

steps.description: device/OS-specific defects are tracked in the issue tracker during the verification cycle
the count of critical device-specific defects exceeds 2
the verification result is marked as failed
release approval is blocked until defects are addressed

US-160.name: Verify user storage limits
US-160.description:
As a QA Engineer
