I want to Verify per-user storage limits are enforced and exceedance is prevented across all storage operations
So that Ensures fair resource allocation, protects system stability, and meets contractual storage policies

background.description: a test user exists with a configured per-user storage quota

scenarios.name: Upload within quota succeeds via each client
scenarios.description: Happy path to ensure uploads within limit are accepted across all supported APIs/clients

steps.description: the user has <used_bytes> bytes already stored out of a <quota_bytes> byte quota
the user uploads <upload_bytes> bytes using the <client_api> client
the upload is accepted with a success response
the total stored bytes equals <expected_total_bytes> and is within the quota

scenarios.name: Upload reaching exact quota boundary succeeds
scenarios.description: Boundary condition where upload brings total to exactly the quota limit

steps.description: the user has <used_bytes> bytes already stored out of a <quota_bytes> byte quota
the user uploads <upload_bytes> bytes using the <client_api> client
the upload is accepted with a success response
the total stored bytes equals the quota and does not exceed it

scenarios.name: Upload beyond quota is rejected with quota exceeded
scenarios.description: Error scenario ensuring system rejects uploads once limit is exceeded

steps.description: the user has <used_bytes> bytes already stored out of a <quota_bytes> byte quota
the user attempts to upload <upload_bytes> bytes using the <client_api> client
the upload is rejected with error code <error_code> and message <error_message>
the total stored bytes remains <used_bytes> and does not increase

scenarios.name: Consistent quota enforcement across all clients at limit
scenarios.description: Edge case to verify enforcement is consistent across all supported clients

steps.description: the user has reached the quota limit of <quota_bytes> bytes
the user attempts to create or upload data using the <client_api> client
the operation is rejected with error code <error_code> and message <error_message>
quota enforcement is identical for the <client_api> client

US-161.name: Verify message and media size limits
US-161.description:
As a QA Engineer
I want to Verify that the system enforces message and media size limits under normal and boundary conditions
So that Ensures performance and stability by preventing oversized payloads from degrading the system

background.description: message and media size limits are configured in the system
a valid authenticated client is available

scenarios.name: Accept message payloads at or below the configured limit
scenarios.description: Validates successful message submission for payload sizes at and below the limit

steps.description: a message payload of size <payload_size>
the client sends the message
the response status code is 2xx
the server logs indicate the payload was accepted

scenarios.name: Reject message payloads above the configured limit
scenarios.description: Validates error handling for oversized message payloads

steps.description: a message payload of size <payload_size>
the client sends the message
the response status code is <error_code>
the error message matches the documented oversized payload message

scenarios.name: Accept media uploads at or below the configured limit
scenarios.description: Validates successful media upload for payload sizes at and below the limit

steps.description: a media file of size <media_size>
the client uploads the media file
the response status code is 2xx
the media file is stored successfully

scenarios.name: Reject media uploads above the configured limit gracefully
scenarios.description: Validates error handling and storage behavior for oversized media uploads

steps.description: a media file of size <media_size>
the client uploads the media file
the response status code is <error_code>
no media file is persisted in storage

scenarios.name: Response time meets SLA at maximum allowed sizes
scenarios.description: Validates response time under boundary payload sizes for messages and media

steps.description: a <payload_type> payload of size limit
the client submits the payload and measures response time
the response time is within the agreed SLA
the response status code is 2xx

scenarios.name: System health remains stable after repeated oversized attempts
scenarios.description: Validates consistent rejection and no service degradation under repeated oversized payloads

steps.description: a series of <attempts> oversized payload submissions
the client submits oversized payloads in sequence
each response returns the documented error code and message
system health metrics remain within normal thresholds

US-162.name: US-162 Verify Backup Strategy
US-162.description:
As a DevOps Engineer
I want to Verify that backups are executed frequently and are reliable across regions through scheduled checks and restore tests
So that Ensures data resilience and availability in case of regional failures or data loss

background.description: the backup monitoring system has access to backup schedules, logs, replication reports, and restore test results for the last 30 days

scenarios.name: Validate backup frequency meets 15-minute target for critical data stores
scenarios.description: Happy path verifying backup frequency meets or exceeds the 15-minute target

steps.description: critical data stores have recorded backup timestamps for the last 30 days
I calculate the maximum interval between consecutive backups for each critical data store
the maximum interval is less than or equal to 15 minutes for each critical data store
no gaps in the backup schedule exceed the target interval

scenarios.name: Cross-region replication success rate meets monthly target
scenarios.description: Happy path verifying replication success rate is at least 99.9% per month

steps.description: monthly replication job reports and failure logs across regions are available
I compute the monthly replication success rate from the reports
the success rate is greater than or equal to 99.9%
any failed replications are documented with remediation status

scenarios.name: Monthly cross-region restore tests achieve 100% success
scenarios.description: Happy path verifying all scheduled restore tests succeed in the month

steps.description: scheduled restore tests were executed for all regions this month
I review the outcomes of each scheduled restore test
all restore tests are marked as successful
the restore evidence includes validation of data integrity

scenarios.name: RPO boundary validation for restore tests
scenarios.description: Boundary condition validating RPO at and above the 15-minute limit

steps.description: restore test results include measured data loss windows
I evaluate the Recovery Point Objective for each restore test
the RPO is within the allowed threshold
any RPO above the threshold is flagged as a failure

scenarios.name: Backup frequency edge case with intermittent delays
scenarios.description: Edge case where backup intervals occasionally approach the 15-minute limit

steps.description: backup logs show intermittent delays for a critical data store
I compute the maximum backup interval over the last 30 days
the maximum interval does not exceed the 15-minute target
intervals within 1 minute of the target are recorded for trend analysis

scenarios.name: Replication report missing data results in analysis failure
scenarios.description: Error scenario where replication data is incomplete

steps.description: replication job reports are missing for one or more regions
I attempt to calculate the monthly replication success rate
the calculation fails due to incomplete data
an alert is raised to request missing reports

scenarios.name: Restore test failure is detected and reported
scenarios.description: Error scenario where a scheduled restore test fails

steps.description: a scheduled cross-region restore test has a failed outcome
I review the restore test outcomes for the month
the monthly restore success rate is below 100%
the failure is logged with root cause and corrective action

US-163.name: Verify RPO/RTO targets documentation, approval, and DR validation
US-163.description:
As a DevOps Engineer
I want to Verify that defined RPO and RTO targets for catastrophic failure are documented, approved, and validated through disaster recovery testing.
So that Ensures the organization can meet business continuity objectives and minimize data loss and downtime after catastrophic events.

background.description: the disaster recovery policy repository and approval records are accessible

scenarios.name: RPO and RTO targets are documented and approved by primary and secondary stakeholders
scenarios.description: Validates policy documentation and approvals for both RPO and RTO

steps.description: the system is under review of disaster recovery policy and approval records
measuring RPO target definition
the RPO is documented and approved by primary and secondary stakeholders
the RTO is documented and approved by primary and secondary stakeholders

scenarios.name: DR simulation meets documented RPO and RTO targets
scenarios.description: Validates achieved RPO and RTO do not exceed documented targets during full DR simulation

steps.description: a full disaster recovery simulation is completed with measured data loss and restore time
comparing achieved RPO and RTO to the documented targets
achieved values are less than or equal to documented targets

scenarios.name: Boundary condition where achieved RPO and RTO equal documented targets
scenarios.description: Ensures equality is accepted for RPO and RTO achievement

steps.description: a full disaster recovery simulation is completed with measured data loss and restore time
comparing achieved RPO and RTO to the documented targets
achieved RPO equals documented RPO
achieved RTO equals documented RTO

scenarios.name: Error when RPO is documented but missing secondary stakeholder approval
scenarios.description: Fails when approval records are incomplete

steps.description: the system is under review of disaster recovery policy and approval records
measuring RPO target definition with missing secondary approval
the RPO target definition does not meet approval requirements

scenarios.name: Error when achieved RPO or RTO exceeds documented targets in DR test
scenarios.description: Fails when DR test outcomes exceed documented RPO or RTO
